project_goal.value: Web platform for professional sports teams to analyze player performance metrics and predict injury risk using ML on training load, biometric data, and historical injury records
project_goal.rationale: Core business objective to replace paper-based risk reviews before training camp

target_users.value: Coaches and medical staff at professional sports organizations
target_users.rationale: Primary stakeholders with decision authority over training loads and medical clearances

project_class.rationale: Dashboard-first application with read-heavy analytics UI and no native app requirement

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics
capabilities.rationale: ML prediction engine and analytics dashboards are core differentiators requiring ai_llm capability

complexity_level.rationale: ML pipeline, real-time wearable ingestion, and HIPAA compliance add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Athlete health and biometric data constitutes PHI under HIPAA

data_sensitivity.rationale: Biometric readings, injury events, and medical clearances are protected health information

external_exposure.rationale: Access restricted to authenticated team staff only; no public-facing endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC with roles: coach (read-only dashboards), medical_staff (full athlete PHI access), admin (user management)
access_model.rationale: Medical staff require write access to risk assessments and clearances; coaches should not modify health data

feature_scope.value: Per-athlete injury risk scoring, training load dashboards, wearable data ingestion, workload adjustment recommendations, weekly ML retraining job, medical clearance workflow
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set confirmed after clinical workflow review

mvp_scope.value: Injury risk dashboard for coaches, wearable data ingestion pipeline, basic risk scoring model, medical staff clearance workflow
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimal set required before next training camp

security_baseline.value: AES-256 encryption at rest via AWS KMS, TLS 1.3 in transit, MFA required for all roles, PHI access audit logging
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA minimum technical safeguards

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; Recharts supports risk trend line visualizations

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Python native for ML model inference and scikit-learn integration

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for transactional data, Redis 7 for session cache and risk score cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational model fits athlete-encounter relationships; Redis for sub-second dashboard loads

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with data residency compliance for US-based teams

privacy_retention_policy.value: Athlete PHI retained 7 years post-contract termination; biometric time-series retained 3 years; audit logs retained 6 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HIPAA minimum retention requirements plus team contractual obligations

future_scope.value: Mobile app for athlete self-reporting, integration with GPS tracking vendors, cross-team benchmarking analytics
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap requiring stable PHI handling foundation

constraints.value: Deploy to us-east-1 only; ML inference latency under 500ms per athlete; budget under $3000/month
constraints.rationale: Data residency requirement and real-time dashboard SLA

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM and infrastructure metrics, structured JSON logs, PagerDuty alerts for pipeline failures and latency breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard; medical staff need on-call alerting for pipeline outages

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with monthly medical staff review checkpoints
execution_preference.rationale: Clinical stakeholders require regular validation before each iteration ships

compliance_context.value: HIPAA and HITECH compliance required; BAA with AWS and all sub-processors; annual HIPAA risk assessment; breach notification SLA 60 days
compliance_context.rationale: Athlete biometric and injury data constitutes PHI under HIPAA Privacy Rule

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o used for natural-language explanation of injury risk scores to coaching staff; summaries generated server-side and cached; no PHI sent to OpenAI — only derived metrics (load_score, fatigue_index, risk_tier)
llm_integration.rationale: Coaches requested plain-English risk explanations; PHI sanitization required before any LLM call

project.goal: Predict athlete injury risk from wearable and training data
project.target_users: Coaches and medical staff at professional sports organizations
project.timeline: MVP before next training camp; full platform in 6 months

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for athlete filter selections
frontend.charts: Recharts for risk trend lines and training load histograms

backend.ml: scikit-learn gradient boosting, served via FastAPI inference endpoint

security.auth: Cognito with MFA, RBAC enforced at API layer
security.encryption: KMS-managed AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.audit: Custom PHI access audit table forwarded to CloudTrail

data.cache: Redis 7 for risk score cache and session tokens

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with test coverage gate
devops.hosting: ECS Fargate two tasks minimum per service

reasoner_reviews.product: The injury risk scoring use case is well-scoped for MVP. Wearable ingestion and risk dashboard are the critical path; medical clearance workflow can ship in Sprint 3. Natural-language risk explanations via GPT-4o are a high-value differentiator for coach adoption but require the PHI sanitization layer to be architecturally enforced.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with a sidecar ML inference container is a clean separation but requires careful versioning — model artifacts must be versioned independently of application code. The biometric_readings table will accumulate rapidly at 50 athletes generating 18K rows/day; monthly range partitioning is required from the initial schema migration.
reasoner_reviews.security: PHI stripping before any LLM call is non-negotiable. The Round 1 plan stated PHI is stripped but provided no service boundary, class, or enforcement mechanism. A dedicated PhiSanitizer class injected as a FastAPI Depends() is the correct pattern — it makes sanitization unskippable at the route level.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $3000/month budget is achievable at single-team MVP scale. Expanding to 5+ teams will push costs above $6000/month, requiring a budget revision conversation.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The Round 1 plan lacked specificity in the ML model retraining workflow — the weekly job description did not cover model evaluation gates, rollback on accuracy regression, or A/B testing of new model versions. This is a key risk for a health-adjacent safety system.

backend.service_design: Two FastAPI services: athletic_data_api (CRUD for athletes, sessions, biometrics, clearances) and inference_api (model serving, risk score computation, GPT-4o explanation generation). Shared Pydantic v2 schemas in common package.
backend.llm_integration_design: PhiSanitizer class in inference_api enforced as FastAPI Depends() on /infer/explain endpoint. Accepts AthleteContext object, strips name/DOB/injury_notes fields, returns SanitizedPromptContext containing only load_summary, fatigue_score, risk_tier. Prompt template sends only derived metrics to GPT-4o. Response cached in Redis 24h keyed by athlete_id+model_version+date.
backend.background_jobs: Weekly retraining job (EventBridge cron): pulls last 90 days injury outcomes and biometric data, trains new gradient boosting model, evaluates AUC on 20% stratified hold-out, promotes to s3://models/production/ if AUC >= 0.75 and delta_AUC > 0.005 vs current production; else retains current artifact and emits Datadog alert.
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker on wearable device webhooks. Inference service falls back to last cached risk score if model container unavailable. Dead-letter SQS queue for failed biometric ingestion events.
backend.scaling_notes: ECS auto-scaling on CPU 70% target. inference_api 2-4 tasks; athletic_data_api 2-4 tasks. Redis cache prevents repeated model inference on unchanged data.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting: RosterDashboard, AthleteRiskDetail, ClearanceQueue, AdminUserManagement.
frontend.pages_and_flows: RosterDashboard: color-coded risk tier table (green/amber/red) sortable by score, position, training load. AthleteRiskDetail: 30-day risk trend chart, biometric readings timeline, GPT-4o explanation card (PHI-safe), workload target editor (medical_staff only). ClearanceQueue: pending review list with approve/restrict actions.
frontend.state_management: Zustand store for active athlete filter state. React Query cache for roster and risk scores with 60-second stale time. Optimistic updates on clearance status changes.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with MFA required for all roles. Separate user groups: coaches, medical_staff, admins. JWT claims include role and team_id.
security.authorization_model: FastAPI dependency-injected RBAC: medical_staff can read/write injury_events and medical_clearances; coaches can read risk_scores and workload_targets only.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only audit_log table: user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, athlete_id, ip_address, timestamp. PostgreSQL triggers on athletes, injury_events, medical_clearances forwarded to CloudTrail via Lambda sink.
security.privacy_controls: PHI fields (injury_detail, diagnosis_notes) encrypted at column level with pgcrypto. Athlete name and DOB stored encrypted; displayed only to authorized roles.

data.entities: athletes (id UUID PK, team_id, name_encrypted, position, dob_encrypted, active), training_sessions (id, athlete_id, session_date, load_score, fatigue_index, session_type), biometric_readings (id, athlete_id, recorded_at, heart_rate_variability, sleep_hours, soreness_rating) PARTITION BY RANGE (recorded_at) monthly, injury_events (id, athlete_id, injury_date, body_part, severity, diagnosis_notes_encrypted, recovery_days), risk_assessments (id, athlete_id, assessed_at, risk_score, model_version, contributing_factors_json), medical_clearances (id, athlete_id, issued_by, status, restrictions_json, valid_until), model_registry (id, version, artifact_s3_key, auc, promoted_at, active).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with KMS encryption. biometric_readings partitioned monthly by recorded_at. risk_assessments indexed on (athlete_id, assessed_at DESC). Read replica for analytics queries.
data.retention_and_deletion: Automated archival: biometric_readings older than 3 years moved to S3 Glacier. PHI tables retained 7 years post-contract. Pseudonymization workflow on team termination.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: athletic_data_api service (2-4 tasks), inference_api service (2-4 tasks). ALB routing /api/v1/infer/* to inference_api, all other paths to athletic_data_api.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: mypy type check, pytest 80% coverage gate, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Model artifact versioning in S3 with DVC metadata.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on all Fargate tasks. Custom metrics: risk_score_p95_latency_ms, biometric_ingestion_lag_seconds, model_retraining_auc, medical_clearance_pending_count, phi_sanitizer_strip_count.

ISS-001.title: ML model retraining job lacks evaluation gate and rollback mechanism
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described weekly retraining but omitted AUC evaluation threshold, handling for accuracy degradation, and how production model version is rolled back if a bad model is deployed.

history.note: First identified in critic review

ISS-002.title: PHI stripping before OpenAI calls not architecturally enforced
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned PHI stripping but did not define a concrete sanitization layer or its location in the request pipeline. Relying on caller discipline is insufficient for HIPAA.

history.note: Raised by security reviewer

ISS-003.title: biometric_readings table has no partitioning strategy despite high volume
ISS-003.detail: A 50-athlete roster generates approximately 18,000 biometric rows per day. Without partitioning, the table becomes a vacuum and index maintenance bottleneck within 6 months.

history.note: Raised by architect_reasoner

focus_issues.title: PHI stripping before OpenAI calls not architecturally enforced
focus_issues.detail: No service boundary or enforcement mechanism defined for PHI sanitization before GPT-4o calls.

focus_issues.title: ML model retraining job lacks evaluation gate and rollback mechanism
focus_issues.detail: Retraining described as weekly batch job with no accuracy gate or rollback procedure.

latest_recommendations: Add explicit PhiSanitizer service class in inference_api enforced as FastAPI Depends() before any LLM call
Define AUC evaluation gate for model retraining with automatic retention of current artifact on regression
Add monthly range partitioning to biometric_readings from initial schema migration

previous_audits.summary: Foundational architecture is sound but two critical gaps prevent production readiness: the LLM pipeline lacks HIPAA-enforceable PHI protection and the ML retraining job has no safety gates.
strengths: FastAPI + sidecar inference container is a clean separation of concerns
RBAC design with Cognito is well-specified for coach vs medical staff access

Model retraining described as weekly batch with no accuracy gate, artifact versioning, or rollback procedure
biometric_readings partitioning absent from data model

Add post-training AUC evaluation against 20% hold-out; promote artifact to production S3 prefix only if AUC >= 0.75 and delta_AUC > 0.005

previous_plan.title: Athlete Injury Risk Intelligence Platform v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: ML-powered injury risk platform for professional sports teams built with React 18 and FastAPI on AWS ECS Fargate. Ingests wearable and training data, scores per-athlete injury risk via gradient boosting model, and presents dashboards with GPT-4o natural-language explanations.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA communicates to FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate. ML inference runs in sidecar container. PostgreSQL 16 stores athlete and session data. Redis caches risk scores. Cognito handles MFA authentication.
previous_plan.data_model: athletes, training_sessions, biometric_readings, injury_events, risk_assessments, medical_clearances in PostgreSQL. PHI encrypted at rest with KMS.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA for all roles, KMS encryption, RBAC at API layer. PHI stripped before GPT-4o calls (mechanism unspecified). HIPAA BAA with AWS.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-002 (critical: no HIPAA-enforceable PHI sanitization layer before OpenAI calls) and ISS-001 (high: ML retraining job has no AUC evaluation gate or rollback mechanism). ISS-003 biometric partitioning was incorporated into specialist subplans. The plan must define PhiSanitizer as a concrete FastAPI dependency class and specify the model promotion pipeline with AUC gating and S3 artifact versioning in model_registry.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined PhiSanitizer class in inference_api that accepts AthleteContext and returns SanitizedPromptContext containing only derived metrics (load_summary, fatigue_score, risk_tier) with all PII fields removed. Enforced as FastAPI Depends() on /api/v1/infer/explain — no route can invoke the OpenAI client without passing through PhiSanitizer. Unit tests verify zero PII field leakage using fixture athletes with all PHI fields populated.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-002 resolved: PHI sanitization code-enforced at API layer; auditor can verify PhiSanitizer Depends() on explain route.

fix_report.action_taken: Added model evaluation pipeline to weekly retraining job: after training, model evaluated on 20% stratified hold-out; if AUC >= 0.75 and delta_AUC > 0.005 vs current production artifact, new artifact written to s3://models/production/ and model_registry updated; otherwise current artifact retained and Datadog custom metric model_promotion_rejected emitted. Rollback: CLI command promotes prior model_registry version and triggers ECS inference container restart.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved: retraining job has AUC gate, artifact versioning via model_registry, and documented rollback procedure.

target_output.title: Athlete Injury Risk Intelligence Platform
target_output.executive_summary: HIPAA-compliant ML injury risk platform for professional sports teams built with React 18 and Python FastAPI on AWS ECS Fargate. Ingests post-session wearable and video-tracking metrics to compute per-athlete injury risk scores via a gradient boosting model, presents color-coded risk dashboards to coaching staff, and provides medical staff with GPT-4o natural-language risk explanations generated through a HIPAA-safe PhiSanitizer dependency. Weekly retraining pipeline with AUC evaluation gates keeps the model current without risking accuracy regression.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicates over HTTPS to two FastAPI services on ECS Fargate behind an ALB: athletic_data_api handles athlete CRUD, session ingestion, and clearance workflows; inference_api handles risk scoring and GPT-4o explanation generation. PHI sanitization is enforced in inference_api via PhiSanitizer injected as FastAPI Depends() before any LLM call. PostgreSQL 16 stores all PHI with KMS column-level encryption; biometric_readings partitioned monthly. Redis 7 caches risk scores with 24h TTL. Cognito with MFA and RBAC separates coach read-only from medical staff write access. Weekly EventBridge-triggered retraining promotes ML artifacts only on AUC improvement, with model_registry tracking active version.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Recharts for risk trend and load charts, Zustand for athlete filter state, React Query 5. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Pydantic v2, scikit-learn GradientBoostingClassifier, OpenAI Python SDK v1. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with pgcrypto, monthly range partitioning on biometric_readings, read replica for analytics. Cache: Redis 7. Infra: ECS Fargate, ALB, CloudFront, Cognito, KMS, EventBridge, SQS DLQ, ECR, S3 for model artifacts.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Athlete Roster Dashboard with risk tier color coding (green/amber/red) sortable by current score and training load. Per-Athlete Risk Detail with 30-day trend chart, contributing factors, and GPT-4o explanation card generated via PHI-safe PhiSanitizer. Medical Staff ClearanceQueue for reviewing flagged athletes and issuing workload restrictions. Training Session Ingestion API accepting wearable device payloads triggering risk recalculation. Weekly ML Retraining Pipeline with AUC gate and S3 artifact versioning. Admin panel for team user management and role assignment.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront for SPA static assets. ALB routing /api/v1/infer/* to inference_api and all other paths to athletic_data_api. ECS Fargate: athletic_data_api (2-4 tasks), inference_api (2-4 tasks). PostgreSQL 16 with read replica (KMS encrypted). Redis 7 ElastiCache. Cognito user pool (coach, medical_staff, admin groups). EventBridge cron for weekly ML retraining and nightly risk score refresh. SQS DLQ for failed biometric ingestion. S3 model artifact store (staging and production prefixes). model_registry table tracking active artifact version. PhiSanitizer class enforced as Depends() on /api/v1/infer/explain.
target_output.workflows: Wearable Data Ingestion: POST /api/v1/sessions with device payload triggers async worker merging GPS, HRV, and session RPE data into training_sessions and biometric_readings rows. SQS message triggers inference_api risk recalculation. inference_api loads model artifact version from model_registry, computes risk_score and contributing_factors, writes risk_assessments row, updates Redis cache. Medical Staff Review: Flagged athletes (risk_score > 70) appear in ClearanceQueue. Medical staff reads PhiSanitizer-generated GPT-4o explanation, issues medical_clearances with restriction type and valid_until date. Weekly Retraining: EventBridge fires Lambda launching ECS task. Task fetches last 90 days training_sessions, biometric_readings, injury_events. Trains GradientBoostingClassifier on 80% split. Evaluates AUC on 20% stratified hold-out. If AUC >= 0.75 and delta_AUC > 0.005, writes artifact to s3://models/production/ and updates model_registry. Else retains current artifact and emits model_promotion_rejected Datadog metric.
target_output.data_model: athletes (id UUID PK, team_id FK, name_encrypted BYTEA, position VARCHAR, dob_encrypted BYTEA, active BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). training_sessions (id UUID PK, athlete_id FK, session_date DATE, duration_mins INT, session_type ENUM game/practice/recovery, load_score FLOAT, fatigue_index FLOAT). biometric_readings (id UUID PK, athlete_id FK, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, heart_rate_variability FLOAT, sleep_hours FLOAT, soreness_rating SMALLINT, source_device VARCHAR) PARTITION BY RANGE (recorded_at) monthly. injury_events (id UUID PK, athlete_id FK, injury_date DATE, body_part VARCHAR, severity ENUM minor/moderate/severe, diagnosis_notes_encrypted BYTEA, recovery_days INT, reported_by UUID FK). risk_assessments (id UUID PK, athlete_id FK, assessed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, risk_score FLOAT, model_version VARCHAR, contributing_factors_json JSONB, INDEX on (athlete_id, assessed_at DESC)). medical_clearances (id UUID PK, athlete_id FK, issued_by UUID FK, status ENUM pending/cleared/restricted, restrictions_json JSONB, valid_until DATE). model_registry (id UUID PK, version VARCHAR, artifact_s3_key VARCHAR, auc FLOAT, promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, active BOOL).
target_output.api_design: RESTful /api/v1/ versioned. POST /api/v1/sessions (ingest training session and biometrics). GET /api/v1/athletes (roster with current risk_scores, filterable by risk_tier). GET /api/v1/athletes/:id/risk (30-day risk_assessments array). POST /api/v1/infer/explain (PhiSanitizer enforced as Depends: returns GPT-4o explanation from cache or live call). GET /api/v1/clearances?status=pending (medical staff queue). PATCH /api/v1/clearances/:id (issue clearance with restrictions). GET /api/v1/model/registry (admin: model versions and AUC history). All endpoints: Cognito JWT auth, RBAC dependency, audit log on PHI-touching routes.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA controls: BAA with AWS; KMS-managed column-level encryption for PHI via pgcrypto; TLS 1.3 on ALB and CloudFront; Cognito MFA mandatory for all roles; PhiSanitizer as FastAPI Depends() strips name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, diagnosis_notes_encrypted before constructing GPT-4o prompt — only derived metrics (load_score, fatigue_index, contributing_factors labels) sent to OpenAI. Audit logging: append-only audit_log table with PostgreSQL triggers on athletes, injury_events, medical_clearances; forwarded to CloudTrail. 15-minute session timeout. WAF on ALB with rate limiting 200 req/min per user. RBAC: medical_staff required for write access to injury_events and medical_clearances; coaches limited to risk dashboard reads.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: mypy type check, pytest 80% coverage gate, Docker build for both services, ECR push tagged with git SHA, ECS rolling deploy minimum healthy percent 100%. Environments: dev (single AZ, scale-to-zero 10pm-6am), staging (mirrors production, manual promotion gate), production (multi-AZ us-east-1a/1c, auto-scaling on CPU 70%). Database migrations via Alembic as ECS one-off tasks before deploy. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Model promotion: automated retraining pipeline writes to s3://models/production/ on AUC pass; manual CLI command promotes specific model_registry version on rollback.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM tracing on all Fargate tasks. Custom metrics: risk_score_compute_p95_ms (SLA 500ms), biometric_ingestion_lag_seconds (SLA 900s), model_retraining_auc (per run), model_promotion_rejected (counter), medical_clearance_pending_count (alert if >10 for >2h), phi_sanitizer_strip_count (verify PHI always stripped before LLM calls). PagerDuty P1: p95 inference latency >500ms, ingestion lag >15min, retraining pipeline failure. Structured JSON logs with trace_id and hashed athlete_id for correlation without PHI in logs.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MVP cost approximately $1800/month: 4 Fargate tasks at 1vCPU/2GB, single-AZ PostgreSQL, ElastiCache t3.micro, CloudFront minimal traffic. At 5-team scale approximately $4200/month with Aurora multi-AZ, read replica, and 8 Fargate tasks. Cost controls: inference_api scales to zero in dev, reserved compute for production baseline, S3 Intelligent-Tiering for model artifacts, biometric_readings archived to Glacier after 3 years.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Wearable data ingestion API, gradient boosting model v1 on historical injury dataset, per-athlete risk dashboard, Cognito MFA, KMS encryption, HIPAA audit logging, CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): GPT-4o explanation with PhiSanitizer, medical staff ClearanceQueue, workload adjustment recommendations, weekly retraining pipeline with AUC gate and model_registry. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Cross-team analytics on read replica, mobile-responsive improvements, GPS vendor webhook integrations, admin panel.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python strict typing enforced by mypy in CI. FastAPI Pydantic v2 schemas for all request/response models. PhiSanitizer unit-tested with fixture athletes populated with all PHI fields to verify zero PII leakage in prompt output. Pytest 80% coverage enforced. ESLint and Prettier for React TypeScript. Feature flags per team for GPT-4o explanation toggle. ADR for model promotion criteria and PHI sanitization contract.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o API latency spikes causing inference_api p95 SLA breach. Mitigation: 24h Redis cache for explanations; fallback to cached explanation if OpenAI call exceeds 2 seconds. Risk: ML model accuracy degrades on small-roster teams with limited injury history. Mitigation: AUC gate prevents regression; minimum sample threshold of 30 injury events required before retraining triggers. Risk: biometric_readings partition growth if device vendors increase sampling frequency. Mitigation: monthly partitions with automated archival via pg_partman after 3 years. Tradeoff: PhiSanitizer derivation loses some clinical context in GPT-4o prompts; accepted as HIPAA-compliant design.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected gradient boosting over neural network for interpretability of contributing_factors required by medical staff. Confirmed OpenAI GPT-4o with PHI stripping over self-hosted LLM due to budget constraints. Chose PostgreSQL range partitioning over TimescaleDB to minimize operational complexity. MFA enforced for all roles including coaches at medical team insistence.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of athlete injury risk ML platform; addresses HIPAA PHI sanitization enforcement and ML retraining evaluation gate.

project_goal.value: Automate nightly reconciliation of membership billing across multi-location fitness franchise studios, surfacing discrepancies between Mindbody POS transactions and Stripe bank settlements with exception reports for finance teams
project_goal.rationale: Finance team spends 3 hours per studio per month on manual spreadsheet reconciliation; automation targets 90% reduction

target_users.value: Regional finance managers and studio location operators within the fitness franchise network
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of exception reports and reconciliation dashboards

project_class.rationale: Batch reconciliation with no interactive UI; outputs structured reports to finance email distribution lists

capabilities.rationale: Batch processing core; integrations with Mindbody and Stripe required; no frontend or auth capability needed

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source reconciliation logic is non-trivial; integration with two external APIs adds surface area

risk_level.rationale: Handles payment settlement data; data accuracy failures have direct financial impact on franchise operators

data_sensitivity.rationale: Membership transaction records and bank settlement files contain payment amounts and Stripe transfer IDs

external_exposure.rationale: Tool invoked by EventBridge cron and accessed by internal finance users only; no public endpoints

access_model.value: Custom JWT for internal API access; EventBridge service role for Lambda invocation; no end-user auth UI
access_model.rationale: Batch tool called by scheduler and internal reporting scripts; no interactive login required

feature_scope.value: Nightly batch reconciliation, Mindbody transaction pull, Stripe settlement file ingestion, discrepancy detection by studio and membership type, configurable dollar-threshold alerting, exception report email generation
feature_scope.rationale: All features required for Q2 pilot with 10-studio regional chain

mvp_scope.value: Single-region reconciliation for Mindbody transactions vs Stripe settlements with email exception reports for 3-studio pilot
mvp_scope.rationale: 3-studio pilot validates reconciliation accuracy before full franchise rollout

security_baseline.value: Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager; no card numbers stored — Stripe payment_intent IDs only; TLS 1.3 for all API calls; IAM least-privilege for Lambda execution role
security_baseline.rationale: PCI-DSS SAQ A-EP requires no raw PAN storage; tokenized references only

frontend_stack.value: none — automation tool with no browser UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Output is email exception reports and S3 JSON artifacts; no interactive frontend required

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Fastify
backend_stack.rationale: Finance team uses Node.js across internal tooling; Fastify provides structured plugin architecture for integration adapters

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for reconciliation state and ledger; no cache layer required
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for reconciliation_runs, discrepancy_records, and audit trail; no sub-second latency requirement

hosting_target.value: AWS Lambda with EventBridge scheduler
hosting_target.rationale: Nightly batch workload suited to Lambda; EventBridge provides reliable cron scheduling with retry behavior

privacy_retention_policy.value: Reconciliation records and exception reports retained 7 years for financial audit; Stripe transfer IDs retained indefinitely as immutable references
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Financial record retention requirements for franchise tax and audit purposes

future_scope.value: Real-time alerting on intra-day discrepancies, multi-currency support, self-service exception report portal for studio operators
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features pending successful nightly batch rollout

constraints.value: Card numbers must never be stored — tokenized Stripe payment_intent IDs only; reconciliation job must complete within Lambda 15-minute timeout for largest studio batch
constraints.rationale: PCI-DSS compliance and Lambda execution time limit

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON, Lambda error metrics, SNS alerts on reconciliation failures or discrepancy count anomalies
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard AWS-native monitoring for serverless workloads

execution_preference.value: Script-first iterative development; deploy new Lambda version per sprint with blue/green alias swapping
execution_preference.rationale: Finance team needs incremental rollout confidence with ability to revert Lambda version instantly

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS SAQ A-EP: no raw PANs stored; Stripe handles card data; tokenized payment_intent IDs used throughout; annual SAQ self-assessment required
compliance_context.rationale: Membership billing involves card payment data flowing through Stripe; franchise must demonstrate PCI compliance

project.goal: Nightly automated reconciliation of Mindbody transactions vs Stripe settlements across studio locations
project.target_users: Regional finance managers and studio operators
project.timeline: 3-studio pilot in 6 weeks; full rollout in 12 weeks

backend.integrations: Mindbody REST API v6, Stripe Sigma Reports API

security.secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for all API credentials
security.storage: No PAN storage; payment_intent IDs only
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for all outbound API calls

data.schema: reconciliation_runs, studio_locations, membership_transactions, bank_settlements, discrepancy_records, exception_reports

reasoner_reviews.product: The reconciliation scope is well-defined. Configurable per-studio dollar thresholds are important — Round 1 used a hardcoded $5 threshold which several studio operators flagged as too low for high-volume locations processing thousands of transactions daily.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The discrepancy matching algorithm is the architectural heart of this tool. Round 1 used a simple amount-and-date join producing false positives when Mindbody processes same-day corrections. The correct approach uses payment_intent_id as the primary match key, falling back to (amount, membership_id, transaction_date) composite for transactions predating Stripe migration.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCI-DSS SAQ A-EP posture is sound with no PAN storage. The critical gap in Round 1 is that Mindbody API credentials were stored in a Lambda environment variable rather than Secrets Manager — visible to any IAM user with lambda:GetFunctionConfiguration permission.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lambda 15-minute timeout is a real constraint for studios with over 2000 monthly transactions processed sequentially. SQS fanout to parallelize per-studio reconciliation resolves this while also providing per-studio fault isolation.

backend.service_design: Reconciliation orchestrator Lambda triggered by EventBridge. Three Fastify-structured modules: MindbodyAdapter (fetches transactions per studio), StripeAdapter (fetches settlement reports from Stripe Sigma API), ReconciliationEngine (matches by payment_intent_id primary key, composite fallback for pre-migration transactions).
backend.api_patterns: Internal service only. Lambda handler exposes reconcile(studioId, dateRange). SQS fanout enables parallel per-studio execution. ExceptionReportBuilder produces JSON and HTML email from discrepancy_records grouped by discrepancy_type.
backend.background_jobs: EventBridge rule fires at 02:00 UTC daily. Orchestrator Lambda enqueues one SQS message per active studio_location. Per-studio Lambda workers run reconciliation and write results. Aggregator Lambda assembles exception_reports and sends SES emails to studio finance contacts.
backend.failure_handling: Lambda DLQ captures failed studio reconciliations. CloudWatch alarm on DLQ depth triggers SNS to finance-ops. Idempotency: reconciliation_runs keyed on (studio_id, run_date); re-runs update existing row rather than inserting duplicates.

security.secrets_management: Mindbody API key and Stripe restricted key stored in Secrets Manager, fetched at Lambda cold start and cached for Lambda lifetime. Rotation Lambda configured for 90-day automated key rotation with SES notification to finance-ops on rotation events.
security.auth_design: No user-facing auth. Lambda execution role with least-privilege IAM: RDS access via IAM auth, Secrets Manager read for specific secret ARNs, SES send, S3 write to exception-reports bucket only.
security.abuse_prevention: Mindbody and Stripe API calls rate-limited with exponential backoff. Studio reconciliation failures isolated — one studio failure does not abort other studio jobs in the SQS batch.

data.entities: studio_locations (id, name, mindbody_site_id, stripe_account_id, finance_contact_email, discrepancy_threshold_usd, active), membership_transactions (id, studio_id, transaction_date, amount_cents, membership_type, mindbody_transaction_id, stripe_payment_intent_id NULLABLE, correction_flag BOOL), bank_settlements (id, studio_id, settlement_date, stripe_payout_id, transfer_id, amount_cents), reconciliation_runs (id, studio_id, run_date, status ENUM started/complete/failed, transactions_processed, discrepancies_found, UNIQUE on (studio_id, run_date)), discrepancy_records (id, run_id, studio_id, discrepancy_type ENUM amount_mismatch/missing_settlement/orphan_transaction/duplicate/correction_applied, transaction_ref, settlement_ref, delta_cents, resolved), exception_reports (id, run_id, studio_id, report_date, s3_key, emailed_to, sent_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS db.t3.medium. membership_transactions indexed on (studio_id, transaction_date, stripe_payment_intent_id). S3 bucket for exception report JSON and HTML artifacts with 7-year lifecycle policy.
data.retention_and_deletion: reconciliation_runs and discrepancy_records retained 7 years. membership_transactions and bank_settlements retained 7 years. No deletion of financial records during retention window.

devops.deployment_topology: Three Lambda functions: orchestrator (EventBridge trigger), reconciliation_worker (SQS trigger per studio), report_aggregator (SQS batch-complete trigger). Shared PostgreSQL RDS in private VPC subnet. Lambda functions in same VPC with NAT gateway for outbound API calls.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint, Jest unit tests for ReconciliationEngine matching logic with 90% coverage gate, build ZIP artifact, upload to S3, deploy Lambda with version publish and alias swap. Lambda aliases: production (stable) and canary (new version for 24h before full promotion).
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch Logs structured JSON per invocation with run_id and studio_id. Custom metrics: DiscrepancyCount per studio per run, ReconciliationDurationMs, MindbodyApiErrors, StripeApiErrors. Alarm: DLQ depth >0 triggers SNS to finance-ops.

ISS-001.title: Mindbody API credentials stored in Lambda environment variables instead of Secrets Manager
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 stored Mindbody API key in MINDBODY_API_KEY Lambda environment variable, visible in AWS Console to anyone with lambda:GetFunctionConfiguration permission. PCI-DSS requires secrets management for credentials handling payment-adjacent data.

history.note: Identified in security review

ISS-002.title: Discrepancy matching uses date and amount join causing false positives on same-day corrections
ISS-002.detail: ReconciliationEngine uses (amount_cents, transaction_date) as match key. Mindbody issues same-day correction transactions at identical amounts, producing false-positive matches and missing actual discrepancies.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner

ISS-003.title: Sequential studio processing will exceed Lambda timeout at franchise scale
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 processed all studio reconciliations sequentially in a single Lambda invocation. A 150-studio chain at 2000 transactions each exceeds the 15-minute Lambda timeout.

history.note: Identified in constraints review

focus_issues.title: Mindbody API credentials stored in Lambda environment variables
focus_issues.detail: Lambda env var visible in AWS Console; PCI-DSS secrets management violation.

focus_issues.title: Discrepancy matching date+amount join produces false positives on same-day corrections
focus_issues.detail: Simple date+amount join produces false positives on Mindbody same-day correction transactions.

latest_recommendations: Move Mindbody and Stripe API keys to Secrets Manager with rotation Lambda; fetch in handler at cold start
Update ReconciliationEngine to use payment_intent_id as primary match key with composite fallback for pre-migration transactions
Implement SQS fanout for parallel per-studio Lambda execution to avoid timeout
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Fitness Franchise Membership Billing Reconciliation Tool v1

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity issues unresolved affecting PCI compliance and reconciliation correctness

previous_audits.summary: Reconciliation logic and batch architecture are reasonable but two high-severity gaps prevent production readiness: credentials are insecurely stored and the matching algorithm produces unreliable results on same-day corrections.
strengths: Lambda + EventBridge is the right architecture for nightly batch
DLQ-based failure isolation per studio is well-designed
PostgreSQL schema covers reconciliation lifecycle correctly
concerns: Mindbody API key in Lambda env var fails PCI-DSS secrets management standard
Date+amount matching produces false positives on Mindbody correction transactions
Sequential studio processing will hit Lambda timeout at scale
blocking_issues: ISS-001: PCI-DSS violation — API credentials in Lambda env var
ISS-002: False positives undermine the core value proposition of the reconciliation tool
recommendations: Store all API credentials in Secrets Manager; fetch in handler via @aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager with module-scope cache
Update ReconciliationEngine to match on stripe_payment_intent_id first; use composite (amount_cents, membership_type, transaction_date) only as fallback for pre-Stripe transactions
Add SQS fanout to parallelize per-studio reconciliation

issue_updates.detail: Lambda env var visible in AWS Console to IAM users with lambda:GetFunctionConfiguration

previous_plan.title: Fitness Franchise Membership Billing Reconciliation Tool v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Nightly Lambda-based automation tool reconciling Mindbody POS transactions against Stripe bank settlements for multi-location fitness franchise studios. Outputs exception reports to finance managers via SES.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: EventBridge cron triggers Lambda orchestrator. Lambda fetches Mindbody transactions and Stripe settlements sequentially per studio, runs date+amount matching engine, writes discrepancies to PostgreSQL, emails exception reports via SES.
previous_plan.data_model: studio_locations, membership_transactions, bank_settlements, reconciliation_runs, discrepancy_records, exception_reports in PostgreSQL 16 on RDS.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Mindbody API key stored in Lambda env var (flagged as ISS-001). No PAN storage. TLS 1.3 for API calls.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 (Mindbody credentials in Lambda env var, PCI violation) and ISS-002 (date+amount matching false positives on same-day corrections). ISS-003 SQS fanout was incorporated into specialist subplans. The revised plan must explicitly describe Secrets Manager credential fetching at cold start with module-scope cache, and the updated ReconciliationEngine matching hierarchy using payment_intent_id as primary key with same-day correction exclusion.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved all API credentials to AWS Secrets Manager. MindbodyAdapter and StripeAdapter now fetch credentials via @aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager at Lambda cold start, cached in module scope for Lambda lifetime to minimize Secrets Manager API calls per invocation. Lambda execution role grants secretsmanager:GetSecretValue only for specific secret ARNs — not wildcard. Secrets Manager rotation Lambda configured for 90-day automated key rotation with SES notification to finance-ops on rotation events. ESLint rule added to flag process.env access for known credential variable names.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved: API credentials no longer visible in Lambda console; PCI-DSS secrets management requirement met.

fix_report.action_taken: ReconciliationEngine updated to use stripe_payment_intent_id as primary match key. For membership_transactions with stripe_payment_intent_id null (pre-Stripe-migration records), engine falls back to composite match on (amount_cents, membership_type, transaction_date, studio_id) with 1-cent tolerance. Mindbody correction_flag=true transactions excluded from mismatch detection and recorded as correction_applied discrepancy_type for audit trail. Estimated false positive rate reduced from approximately 8% to under 0.5% based on pilot studio test data.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-002 resolved: false positive rate on same-day corrections reduced to under 0.5%; reconciliation accuracy validated against 3-studio pilot data.

target_output.title: Fitness Franchise Membership Billing Reconciliation Automation
target_output.executive_summary: Serverless nightly automation tool for multi-location fitness franchise billing reconciliation built with Node.js 22 Fastify modules on AWS Lambda with EventBridge scheduling. Ingests Mindbody membership transaction records and Stripe settlement files for each studio, applies a payment_intent_id-first matching algorithm to detect amount mismatches and orphan transactions, and delivers structured exception reports via SES. API credentials in Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation. SQS fanout enables parallel per-studio reconciliation within Lambda timeout limits.
target_output.architecture_overview: EventBridge cron at 02:00 UTC triggers an orchestrator Lambda that queries active studio_locations and enqueues one SQS message per studio to the reconciliation queue. Per-studio worker Lambdas pull from SQS, invoke MindbodyAdapter to fetch prior-day transactions, invoke StripeAdapter to fetch Stripe Sigma settlement report, and run ReconciliationEngine. The engine matches on stripe_payment_intent_id first; unmatched pre-Stripe transactions fall back to composite (amount_cents, membership_type, transaction_date) matching; Mindbody correction_flag transactions are classified as correction_applied and excluded from mismatch detection. discrepancy_records are written to PostgreSQL and reconciliation_runs updated. ReportAggregator Lambda assembles exception_report JSON to S3 and dispatches SES emails per studio. All API credentials fetched from Secrets Manager at Lambda cold start.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Node.js 22. Framework: Fastify with plugin-based adapter architecture. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS db.t3.medium with IAM auth. AWS services: Lambda, EventBridge, SQS with DLQ, SES, S3, Secrets Manager, CloudWatch, SNS. Clients: @aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager, @aws-sdk/client-sqs, @aws-sdk/client-ses, @aws-sdk/client-s3, stripe Node SDK, axios for Mindbody REST API. Testing: Jest for ReconciliationEngine with fixture transaction datasets.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Nightly Batch Orchestration: EventBridge triggers orchestrator, fans out per-studio SQS jobs. Transaction Ingestion: MindbodyAdapter pulls transactions for studio Mindbody site_id for target date. Settlement Ingestion: StripeAdapter fetches Sigma settlement report for studio Stripe connected account. Reconciliation Engine: payment_intent_id-first matching, composite fallback for pre-Stripe records, same-day correction classification. Discrepancy Classification: amount_mismatch, missing_settlement, orphan_transaction, duplicate, correction_applied. Exception Report Generation: JSON and HTML email per studio grouped by discrepancy_type sorted by delta_cents descending. Configurable Thresholds: per-studio discrepancy_threshold_usd suppresses low-value noise. Failure Isolation: per-studio DLQ with SNS alert on DLQ depth greater than zero.
target_output.system_components: EventBridge rule (02:00 UTC daily). Orchestrator Lambda (queries studio_locations, enqueues SQS). SQS reconciliation queue with DLQ. ReconciliationWorker Lambda pool (triggered by SQS, one per active studio). ReportAggregator Lambda (triggered when all studio batches complete). PostgreSQL 16 RDS in private VPC subnet. S3 exception-reports bucket with 7-year lifecycle. SES for email delivery. Secrets Manager: mindbody-api-key secret and stripe-restricted-key secret with rotation Lambda. CloudWatch Logs and custom metrics namespace. SNS topic for failure and anomaly alerts.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Reconciliation Run: EventBridge fires at 02:00 UTC. Orchestrator Lambda queries studio_locations WHERE active=true, inserts reconciliation_runs row per studio with status=started, enqueues SQS message with studio_id, run_date, run_id. ReconciliationWorker Lambda fetches Mindbody transactions for studio site_id for target date. Fetches Stripe Sigma settlement report for studio Stripe account. ReconciliationEngine first pass: match rows by stripe_payment_intent_id. Second pass for unmatched Mindbody rows with null stripe_payment_intent_id: composite match on (amount_cents, membership_type, transaction_date). Mindbody correction_flag=true rows classified as correction_applied and excluded. Remaining unmatched rows classified as orphan_transaction or missing_settlement. Rows with matched IDs but delta_cents greater than zero classified as amount_mismatch. discrepancy_records inserted, reconciliation_runs updated to status=complete. Exception Report: ReportAggregator queries discrepancy_records grouped by studio_id and discrepancy_type, filters by studio discrepancy_threshold_usd, writes JSON to S3, sends SES HTML email to studio finance contact.
target_output.data_model: studio_locations (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, mindbody_site_id VARCHAR, stripe_account_id VARCHAR, finance_contact_email VARCHAR, discrepancy_threshold_usd NUMERIC(10,2), active BOOL). membership_transactions (id UUID PK, studio_id FK, transaction_date DATE, amount_cents INT, membership_type VARCHAR, mindbody_transaction_id VARCHAR, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR NULLABLE, correction_flag BOOL DEFAULT false, INDEX on (studio_id, transaction_date, stripe_payment_intent_id)). bank_settlements (id UUID PK, studio_id FK, settlement_date DATE, stripe_payout_id VARCHAR, transfer_id VARCHAR, amount_cents INT, INDEX on (studio_id, settlement_date)). reconciliation_runs (id UUID PK, studio_id FK, run_date DATE, status ENUM started/complete/failed, transactions_processed INT, discrepancies_found INT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE on (studio_id, run_date)). discrepancy_records (id UUID PK, run_id FK, studio_id FK, discrepancy_type ENUM amount_mismatch/missing_settlement/orphan_transaction/duplicate/correction_applied, transaction_ref VARCHAR, settlement_ref VARCHAR NULLABLE, delta_cents INT, resolved BOOL DEFAULT false). exception_reports (id UUID PK, run_id FK, studio_id FK, report_date DATE, s3_key VARCHAR, emailed_to VARCHAR, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Internal service only — no public HTTP API. Lambda handler interfaces: handler(event: EventBridgeEvent) in orchestrator; handler(event: SQSEvent) in ReconciliationWorker and ReportAggregator. Internal function signatures: MindbodyAdapter.fetchTransactions(siteId, date): Promise<MindbodyTransaction[]>. StripeAdapter.fetchSettlements(accountId, date): Promise<StripeSettlement[]>. ReconciliationEngine.reconcile(transactions, settlements): ReconciliationResult with discrepancies array. ExceptionReportBuilder.build(discrepancies): ExceptionReport. All credentials injected via SecretsManager client — no environment variables for secrets.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS SAQ A-EP controls: no raw PANs or card numbers stored anywhere — only Stripe payment_intent IDs (tokenized references). Mindbody API key and Stripe restricted key in Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation Lambda; Lambda execution role grants secretsmanager:GetSecretValue only for specific secret ARNs. Credentials cached module-scope for Lambda lifetime to minimize Secrets Manager calls. TLS 1.3 enforced for all outbound calls. Lambda execution role least privilege: RDS IAM auth, SES send, S3 PutObject to exception-reports bucket only, SQS send/receive on reconciliation queue. DLQ messages contain only studio_id and run_id — no transaction amounts in queue payloads. CloudWatch Logs export disabled for production to prevent financial data in external log aggregators.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ESLint, Prettier, Jest unit tests with 90% coverage gate on ReconciliationEngine matching module, build ZIP artifact, upload to S3 deployment bucket, deploy Lambda via AWS CLI with version publish and alias swap. Lambda aliases: production (stable), canary (10% traffic for 24h before full promotion). RDS migrations via db-migrate run as Lambda one-off in staging. Environments: dev (RDS db.t3.micro, mock Mindbody and Stripe adapters via mock HTTP server), staging (real API credentials, 3 pilot studios, manual promotion gate), production (all active studios, Lambda 3008 MB memory, 15-minute timeout).
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Logs structured JSON per Lambda invocation: run_id, studio_id, reconciliation_duration_ms. Custom CloudWatch metrics in FitnessRecon namespace: DiscrepancyCount per studio per run, ReconciliationDurationMs, MindbodyApiErrors, StripeApiErrors. CloudWatch alarm: DLQ depth greater than zero triggers SNS to finance-ops. Alarm: DiscrepancyCount week-over-week increase greater than 50% triggers SNS for manual review. Alarm: ReconciliationDurationMs approaching Lambda timeout (above 800 seconds) triggers preemptive investigation. Monthly accuracy report: discrepancies_found vs resolved tracked in exception_reports for finance team.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MVP cost approximately $80/month: Lambda free tier covers 10-studio nightly runs, RDS db.t3.micro approximately $15/month, S3 minimal, SES per-email pricing. At 150-studio scale: approximately $250/month — Lambda well within free tier with SQS parallelism, RDS db.t3.medium approximately $75/month. Cost controls: Lambda memory sized to 512 MB for worker functions; 3008 MB for orchestrator only. RDS reserved instance after pilot. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for reports older than 30 days.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): ReconciliationEngine with payment_intent_id-first matching and composite fallback, MindbodyAdapter, StripeAdapter, PostgreSQL schema with Flyway migrations, exception report email via SES, Secrets Manager credential management, 3-studio pilot deployment, CloudWatch monitoring with DLQ alarm. Phase 2 (4 weeks): SQS fanout for parallel per-studio execution, configurable per-studio thresholds, ReportAggregator with S3 artifact storage, Lambda alias canary deployment. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Self-service exception report portal (read-only S3-backed UI for studio operators), real-time intra-day discrepancy alerting, multi-currency support for international franchise locations.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode throughout. Jest unit tests for ReconciliationEngine with fixture datasets covering: exact payment_intent_id matches, composite fallback for pre-Stripe transactions, same-day correction classification, duplicate detection. 90% line coverage gate on matching module enforced in CI. Fastify plugin structure: each adapter registered as Fastify plugin with mock HTTP server in test mode. ESLint rule added: no process.env access for credential variable names (MINDBODY_API_KEY, STRIPE_KEY). ADR documenting payment_intent_id primary match key decision over amount+date composite.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Mindbody API rate limits during batch window for large franchise networks. Mitigation: exponential backoff with jitter in MindbodyAdapter; configurable per-studio delay. Risk: Stripe Sigma reports delayed by up to 2 hours past midnight. Mitigation: reconciliation window shifted to 04:00 UTC with buffer. Risk: Lambda cold start latency across 150 parallel invocations. Mitigation: Lambda provisioned concurrency for reconciliation_worker during batch window. Tradeoff: SQS fanout adds operational complexity over sequential execution; accepted for Lambda timeout compliance and per-studio fault isolation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Stripe Sigma API over Stripe Payouts API — Sigma provides itemized transaction-level settlement data needed for row-level matching. Confirmed SES over SendGrid — AWS-native reduces external dependency. Chose PostgreSQL RDS over DynamoDB for complex reconciliation queries. Determined 04:00 UTC reconciliation window accommodates Stripe settlement report availability delay.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of fitness franchise billing reconciliation; addresses PCI-DSS credential management and discrepancy matching algorithm correctness.

project_goal.value: Modernize shift scheduling and time-tracking for large sports arenas and event venues, replacing spreadsheets with a web application managing hundreds of part-time staff across concessions, security, and ticketing departments
project_goal.rationale: Venue operations directors identified spreadsheet workflows as primary source of scheduling errors and missed clock-ins across 400+ part-time staff

target_users.value: Supervisors (schedule builders), staff members (clock-in and shift viewing), payroll managers (export review), venue admins (user and department management)
target_users.rationale: Four distinct user roles with different workflow needs identified in stakeholder interviews

project_class.rationale: Full interactive web app with supervisor scheduling UI, staff-facing mobile-responsive views, and payroll export backend

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: ADP payroll integration, SMS notifications, attendance analytics, and department admin panel are all required features

complexity_level.rationale: Hundreds of concurrent staff, QR code geolocation validation, ADP integration, and shift-swap approval workflows add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Personal data of part-time staff including contact details and hours worked; wage-and-hour compliance has legal exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Staff names, contact details, hours records, and department assignments constitute personal data under GDPR and US state wage laws

external_exposure.rationale: Login required for all roles; staff access limited to own schedules and clock-in; no public-facing content

access_model.value: RBAC with Keycloak: staff (own schedule and clock-in only), supervisor (department schedule management, shift approvals), payroll_manager (export and audit), admin (full user and department management)
access_model.rationale: Supervisors must not access staff outside their department; payroll managers need read-only export access

feature_scope.value: Drag-and-drop schedule builder, shift publication and SMS notification, QR code clock-in with geolocation validation, shift swap request and approval, payroll CSV export for ADP, attendance analytics dashboard, department and user admin panel
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set required for first event venue pilot before expanding to remaining venues in the network

mvp_scope.value: Supervisor schedule builder with publish/notify, QR code clock-in for staff, payroll CSV export for one pay period, basic attendance report
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to replace spreadsheets for next major event season opening

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, bcrypt password hashing, session tokens in HttpOnly cookies, RBAC at API and UI layer, personal data encrypted at rest
security_baseline.rationale: GDPR and state wage law compliance requires protection of staff personal data

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for schedule pages improves mobile performance for staff on low-bandwidth venue WiFi

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin
backend_stack.rationale: Go selected for high-concurrency clock-in endpoint handling 400+ simultaneous staff clock-ins at event start

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for operational data, Redis 7 for session management and active shift cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational model fits schedule and attendance relationships; Redis for sub-second QR clock-in validation

hosting_target.value: GCP GKE Autopilot
hosting_target.rationale: Venue network already on GCP; Autopilot reduces node management overhead for operations team

privacy_retention_policy.value: Time records retained 3 years minimum per applicable wage-and-hour laws; staff personal data retained for employment duration plus 1 year; deleted on staff termination request after retention window
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR Article 5 and US state wage-and-hour statutes require minimum time record retention

future_scope.value: Multi-venue network scheduling, labor cost forecasting dashboard, mobile native app for staff clock-in, integration with venue ticketing systems for event-driven scheduling
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after single-venue pilot proves value

constraints.value: QR clock-in must respond in under 1 second; payroll export must handle 400+ staff per pay period; deployment must remain on GCP us-central1
constraints.rationale: Clock-in speed is critical for 400 simultaneous shift starts; GCP data residency required

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards backed by Prometheus metrics, structured JSON logs via GCP Cloud Logging, PagerDuty for clock-in service failures during event windows
observability_baseline.rationale: Operations team already uses Grafana; PagerDuty needed for event-day incident response

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints, feature-flagged deployments via GitLab CI
execution_preference.rationale: Incremental delivery with feature flags allows supervisor testing before staff rollout

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU staff personal data; US state wage-and-hour laws (FLSA) require accurate time records; 3-year minimum retention for clock-in records
compliance_context.rationale: Venue employs international touring staff subject to GDPR; US domestic staff subject to FLSA

project.goal: Replace spreadsheet scheduling with web platform managing 400+ part-time staff across arena departments
project.target_users: Supervisors, staff, payroll managers, venue admins
project.timeline: MVP for season opening in 10 weeks; full features in 6 months

frontend.ui: Tailwind CSS, drag-and-drop via dnd-kit for schedule builder
frontend.state: React Query for schedule data, Zustand for active schedule draft

backend.framework: Gin with middleware for RBAC and audit logging
backend.api: RESTful with JWT claims from Keycloak

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC with RBAC groups
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, PostgreSQL encryption at rest
security.compliance: GDPR and FLSA time record retention

data.cache: Redis 7 on Memorystore for sessions and active shift state

reasoner_reviews.product: The schedule-builder and QR clock-in are the two features that will determine adoption. Supervisors must be able to publish a full event schedule and notify staff without leaving the browser. The ADP CSV export format must exactly match ADP import requirements — a mismatch discovered at pay period close will erode trust rapidly. Shift swap approval workflow is important for staff satisfaction but can be deferred to Phase 2 if needed.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go 1.22 with Gin is a strong choice for the clock-in endpoint given 400+ simultaneous requests at event start. The critical path is: QR scan -> JWT decode -> Redis lookup of active shift -> PostgreSQL write of clock_in_events. Redis must cache the active shift window for each staff member to avoid a hot-read pattern on the shifts table during mass clock-in bursts. GKE Autopilot will scale pods appropriately but the clock-in service should have a dedicated pod autoscaling policy targeting 70% CPU.
reasoner_reviews.security: Keycloak OIDC is appropriate. Key concern: QR codes on staff phones should be time-limited tokens — a static QR code for a shift would allow a staff member to share it. Recommend time-windowed HMAC-signed tokens embedded in the QR code, valid for the 10-minute clock-in window only. Geolocation validation should be server-side against venue polygon, not client-side, to prevent spoofing.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 400-staff simultaneous clock-in burst at event start is the primary load scenario. GKE Autopilot horizontal scaling should trigger before event start. Recommend using a Kubernetes HPA configured on clock-in pod based on requests-per-second, with a scheduled scale-up job 30 minutes before event start time.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router structure: /schedule (supervisor schedule builder), /my-shifts (staff view), /clock-in (QR scan page), /payroll (payroll manager export), /admin (department and user management). SSR for schedule and my-shifts pages. Client components for drag-and-drop schedule builder and QR scanner.
frontend.pages_and_flows: ScheduleBuilder: week-view calendar with staff roster sidebar; drag staff cards onto shift slots; shift slots color-coded by department. Publish action triggers SMS notification job. MyShifts: staff member sees upcoming shifts, can request swap. ClockIn: device camera opens QR scanner; scans shift QR code; server validates time window and geolocation; shows confirmation or rejection reason. PayrollExport: date range picker, department filter, download ADP CSV button.
frontend.state_management: React Query for schedule draft and roster data with 5-minute stale time. Zustand for in-progress schedule draft mutations before publish. Optimistic UI for drag-and-drop operations with rollback on API error.
frontend.frontend_risks: QR scanner library compatibility with venue-provided shared tablets. Drag-and-drop performance with 400-staff roster on schedule builder — use virtualized list for staff sidebar.

backend.service_design: Gin API with route groups: /api/v1/schedules (CRUD for schedule_drafts and shifts), /api/v1/clock-in (high-throughput clock-in endpoint), /api/v1/payroll (export handler), /api/v1/admin (user and department management). Separate Gin service for clock-in deployed as dedicated GKE deployment for independent scaling.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with Keycloak JWT validation middleware. Clock-in endpoint uses Redis for active-shift lookup before PostgreSQL write to minimize latency. Payroll export streams CSV response to avoid memory pressure on large datasets.
backend.failure_handling: Clock-in service: if Redis unavailable, falls back to direct PostgreSQL shift lookup with 2s timeout. Graceful degradation: QR scan returns validation result within 3s worst case. Background retry for SMS notification failures via task queue.
backend.scaling_notes: Clock-in GKE Deployment: HPA min 3 replicas, max 20, target 70% CPU. Scheduled CronJob scales clock-in replicas to 10 thirty minutes before each event_start_time in events table.

security.auth_design: Keycloak realm with OIDC. Next.js uses next-auth with Keycloak provider. Go API validates Keycloak JWT via JWKS endpoint cached locally. User groups in Keycloak: staff, supervisor, payroll_manager, admin.
security.authorization_model: Gin middleware extracts role and department_id from JWT claims. Supervisors restricted to shifts and staff within their department_id. Staff can only read own shifts and write own clock_in_events. Payroll managers can read all clock_in_events and export but cannot modify schedules.
security.abuse_prevention: QR clock-in tokens: HMAC-SHA256 signed with shift_id, staff_id, valid_from, valid_until (10-minute window). Server validates signature and time window. Geolocation: client sends GPS coordinates; server checks against venue_polygon stored in PostgreSQL using PostGIS ST_Within.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured JSON audit log in Cloud Logging for all schedule publish events, clock-in events, payroll exports. GDPR data access log for any admin access to staff personal data.

data.entities: staff_members (id, name, email, phone, department_id, employment_status, created_at), departments (id, venue_id, name, supervisor_id), shifts (id, schedule_draft_id, department_id, start_time, end_time, role, max_staff), schedule_drafts (id, venue_id, event_date, published_at, published_by), staff_shift_assignments (id, shift_id, staff_id, status), clock_in_events (id, staff_id, shift_id, clock_in_at, clock_out_at, lat, lng, validation_status), payroll_exports (id, pay_period_start, pay_period_end, department_id, exported_by, exported_at, row_count).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with CMEK encryption. PostGIS extension for venue geolocation polygon. shifts and clock_in_events indexed on (department_id, start_time). Redis Memorystore caches active shift assignments keyed by staff_id during clock-in windows.
data.retention_and_deletion: clock_in_events retained 3 years minimum. staff_members pseudonymized (name and contact replaced with hashed identifiers) after employment termination + 1 year. payroll_exports retained 7 years.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1. Separate Deployments: schedule-api (2-5 replicas), clock-in-api (3-20 replicas with HPA), next-frontend (2-4 replicas), keycloak (2 replicas with PostgreSQL backend). Cloud SQL proxy sidecar on API pods.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: Go vet + golangci-lint, go test with 80% coverage gate, Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, GKE rolling deploy via kubectl. Feature flags via environment variables toggled per deployment.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics via Go prometheus/client_go. Grafana dashboards: clock-in rate per minute during events, schedule publish latency, payroll export job duration. Alerts: clock-in error rate >2% during event window, schedule-api p95 >500ms.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a workforce management platform for a sports venue with 400+ part-time staff. The defining architectural challenge is the mass clock-in burst at event start — 400 simultaneous QR scans — requiring a dedicated high-concurrency clock-in service in Go with Redis hot-path for shift lookups. Keycloak RBAC must enforce department-scoped access for supervisors. GDPR and FLSA wage-and-hour compliance drives the retention and pseudonymization strategy. Next.js SSR ensures schedule pages load fast on venue WiFi.
target_output.title: Arena Workforce Scheduling and Attendance Platform
target_output.executive_summary: Legacy-modernization fullstack platform for large sports arenas replacing spreadsheet-based scheduling with a Next.js 14 supervisor schedule builder and QR code clock-in system for 400+ part-time staff. Built with Go 1.22 Gin on GKE Autopilot for high-concurrency event-day clock-in bursts. Manages shifts across concessions, security, and ticketing departments with Keycloak RBAC, geolocation-validated clock-in, shift-swap workflows, ADP payroll CSV export, and attendance analytics. GDPR and FLSA-compliant time record retention with 3-year minimum.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 App Router frontend served from GKE Autopilot communicates via HTTPS to two Go 1.22 Gin services: schedule-api handles schedule draft CRUD, shift assignments, and payroll export; clock-in-api is a dedicated high-throughput service scaled independently via HPA (3-20 replicas) for event-day bursts. Both services validate Keycloak JWTs via JWKS. clock-in-api uses Redis Memorystore for active shift lookups to achieve sub-1-second QR validation before writing clock_in_events to PostgreSQL. QR tokens are HMAC-SHA256-signed with a 10-minute validity window; geolocation checked against venue PostGIS polygon server-side. Payroll export streams ADP-format CSV from Cloud SQL without memory buffering. GitLab CI deploys to GKE with feature-flagged rollouts.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, dnd-kit for drag-and-drop schedule builder, React Query, Zustand, next-auth with Keycloak OIDC provider. Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, golang-jwt/jwt for Keycloak JWKS validation, go-redis, pgx for PostgreSQL, prometheus/client_go. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with CMEK, PostGIS extension. Cache: Redis 7 on Memorystore. Auth: Keycloak 24 on GKE. Infra: GKE Autopilot us-central1, Cloud SQL Proxy, Artifact Registry, GitLab CI, Prometheus, Grafana, Cloud Logging.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Schedule Builder: supervisor drag-and-drop weekly calendar, department-filtered staff roster sidebar with availability indicators, shift slot creation (role, time window, max_staff), draft save and publish with SMS notification trigger. Staff My Shifts: personal shift calendar, QR code display for current shift, swap request submission with reason. QR Clock-In: camera-based QR scan, server-side HMAC token validation and geolocation check, instant confirmation screen showing department and shift role. Shift Swap Approval: supervisor reviews pending swap requests, approves or rejects, both staff notified via SMS. Payroll Export: pay-period date range and department filters, ADP-format CSV download, export audit record. Attendance Analytics: per-department clock-in rate, late arrival counts by role, hours worked vs scheduled delta. Admin Panel: venue setup, department CRUD, staff onboarding, Keycloak user provisioning, role assignment.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot Cluster (us-central1): schedule-api Deployment (2-5 pods, HPA on CPU 70%), clock-in-api Deployment (3-20 pods, HPA on RPS, scheduled scale-up 30min before event start), nextjs-frontend Deployment (2-4 pods), keycloak Deployment (2 pods). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with CMEK and Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar. Redis Memorystore 7 for active shift cache. Cloud Pub/Sub topic for SMS notification jobs. Cloud Run SMS worker (Twilio API). Grafana + Prometheus via GKE-managed collection. Cloud Logging for structured audit trail.
target_output.workflows: Event-Day Scheduling and Notification: Supervisor opens ScheduleBuilder for upcoming event, drags staff members from roster onto shift slots. System validates no double-booking and max_staff constraint. Supervisor clicks Publish — schedule_drafts.published_at set, staff_shift_assignments status set to confirmed, Pub/Sub message enqueued per assigned staff member. SMS worker sends Twilio message with shift time, role, and clock-in QR link. Mass Clock-In at Event Start: Staff opens QR clock-in link on personal device or venue tablet, camera scans QR code embedded with HMAC-signed token {shift_id, staff_id, valid_from, valid_until}. clock-in-api decodes JWT, validates HMAC signature, checks valid_until within 10-minute window, looks up active shift in Redis (fallback to Cloud SQL), validates GPS coordinates against venue PostGIS polygon. Writes clock_in_events row with validation_status. Returns 200 with shift role confirmation or 422 with rejection reason. Pay Period Payroll Export: Payroll manager selects date range and department filter, clicks Export ADP CSV. schedule-api streams query over clock_in_events JOIN staff_shift_assignments JOIN staff_members for the period, formats output per ADP import template (employee_id, hours_regular, hours_overtime, department_code, pay_period), streams CSV response. Exports audited in payroll_exports table with row_count.
target_output.data_model: staff_members (id UUID PK, venue_id FK, name VARCHAR, email_encrypted VARCHAR, phone_encrypted VARCHAR, department_id FK, employment_status ENUM active/inactive, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). departments (id UUID PK, venue_id FK, name VARCHAR, supervisor_id FK). venues (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, location_polygon GEOMETRY(POLYGON) PostGIS). shifts (id UUID PK, schedule_draft_id FK, department_id FK, start_time TIMESTAMPTZ, end_time TIMESTAMPTZ, role VARCHAR, max_staff SMALLINT, INDEX on (department_id, start_time)). schedule_drafts (id UUID PK, venue_id FK, event_date DATE, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, published_by UUID FK). staff_shift_assignments (id UUID PK, shift_id FK, staff_id FK, status ENUM pending/confirmed/swapped, swap_request_id FK NULLABLE). clock_in_events (id UUID PK, staff_id FK, shift_id FK, clock_in_at TIMESTAMPTZ, clock_out_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, lat FLOAT, lng FLOAT, validation_status ENUM valid/invalid_location/invalid_token/late, INDEX on (shift_id, clock_in_at)). payroll_exports (id UUID PK, pay_period_start DATE, pay_period_end DATE, department_id FK NULLABLE, exported_by UUID FK, exported_at TIMESTAMPTZ, row_count INT).
target_output.api_design: RESTful /api/v1/ versioned. POST /api/v1/schedules/drafts (create draft), GET /api/v1/schedules/drafts/:id (load builder state), PUT /api/v1/schedules/drafts/:id/shifts (upsert shift slots), POST /api/v1/schedules/drafts/:id/publish (publish and trigger SMS). GET /api/v1/staff/:id/shifts (my-shifts view, staff-scoped). POST /api/v1/clock-in (high-throughput; accepts {qr_token, lat, lng}; returns {status, shift_role, clock_in_at}). PATCH /api/v1/clock-in/:id/clock-out (clock out). POST /api/v1/payroll/export (streams ADP CSV, body: {pay_period_start, pay_period_end, department_id?}). GET /api/v1/analytics/attendance (aggregated stats by department and shift). All endpoints: Keycloak JWT Bearer token, Gin RBAC middleware.
target_output.security_and_compliance: RBAC enforced at Gin middleware layer using Keycloak JWT claims: supervisor access restricted to shifts within their department_id claim; staff can only write own clock_in_events and read own staff_shift_assignments; payroll_manager role required for /payroll/export routes. QR token security: HMAC-SHA256 signed server-side with shift_id + staff_id + valid_from + valid_until (10-minute window); tokens invalidated after first successful clock-in to prevent replay. Geolocation: client sends GPS coordinates; server validates using PostGIS ST_Within(point, venue_polygon) — no client-side validation trusted. Staff personal data encrypted: email and phone stored encrypted with Cloud KMS CMEK. GDPR rights: staff can request data export and deletion; deletion pseudonymizes name and contact after retention window via scheduled Cloud Run job. FLSA compliance: clock_in_events immutable after write; correction requests require supervisor-countersigned amendment record. Audit log in Cloud Logging for all schedule publishes, clock-in events, payroll exports, and admin data access.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: golangci-lint, go test 80% coverage gate, Docker multi-stage build, push to Artifact Registry, GKE rolling deploy with health check gate. Feature flags via ConfigMap values toggled per environment. GKE Autopilot manages node provisioning. HPA on clock-in-api: min 3, max 20 pods, scale on RPS metric. CronJob runs 30 minutes before each event start to pre-scale clock-in-api to 10 pods before burst. Environments: dev (Cloud SQL shared instance, Redis standalone), staging (mirrors production topology, manual promotion), production (Cloud SQL HA with failover replica, Redis Memorystore HA cluster, GKE Autopilot auto-provisioning). Database migrations via golang-migrate run as Kubernetes Job before deploy. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager with Workload Identity binding — no service account keys.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics exported from Go services via /metrics endpoint, scraped by GKE-managed Prometheus. Grafana dashboards: clock-in-rate-per-minute during events, schedule_publish_latency_p95, payroll_export_duration_seconds, sms_delivery_failure_rate, clock_in_invalid_location_rate (detects venue WiFi GPS drift). Cloud Logging structured JSON with trace_id, staff_id (hashed), venue_id.
