Update mechanism: Sparkle framework checks for new versions on app launch using the network entitlement already present for Sentry.
target_output.observability: Sentry Swift SDK captures unhandled exceptions and crashes with anonymized stack traces; DSN configured in Info.plist. Custom breadcrumbs logged at timing file import start and end, PDF render start and end, and GRDB migration events to aid crash diagnosis. No production telemetry dashboard - crash volume tracked in Sentry project dashboard. Performance: os_signpost intervals around split delta computation and PDF render to identify regressions in Instruments during development.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero operational cost - no hosted infrastructure. Apple Developer Program: $99/year. Mac App Store distribution: standard commission on paid purchases. Direct download: free GitHub hosting for notarized .dmg. SQLite performance is sufficient for a single coach's 200+ swimmer roster and 3 years of competition history; no scaling concern within the app lifetime.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): SwiftUI NavigationSplitView layout, Omega Timing file parser, split-time breakdown table with personal best delta coloring, basic swimmer roster CRUD, PDF heat sheet export for Letter format, SQLite schema v1 via GRDB migrations, SwiftLint and snapshot CI, TestFlight distribution to 3 pilot coaches. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Colorado Timing file parser, A4 PDF heat sheet layout, competition history browser, performance progression graph using Charts framework, Sparkle auto-update. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Mac App Store submission and review, direct download .dmg packaging, Xcode Cloud production pipeline, App Store privacy declaration.
target_output.development_guidelines: All async work executes on named actors (SplitAnalysisActor, DatabaseActor) - never dispatch_async; enforce with @MainActor annotations on SwiftUI views. GRDB typed records for all database entities - no raw SQL string queries in view models. TimingImporter parsers covered by unit tests with real sample files from Omega and Colorado Timing provided by pilot coaches. PDF layout validated by snapshot tests asserting page dimensions and text placement on both Letter and A4. SwiftLint enforced in CI with no force-unwraps allowed.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Omega and Colorado Timing file formats change with firmware updates, breaking the parser silently. Mitigation: parsers return ImportError.unsupportedFormat with the detected header line when format validation fails; pilot coaches report format issues via Sentry breadcrumbs. Risk: GRDB migration errors on app launch if migration script has bugs. Mitigation: migration tests in CI use an in-memory SQLite instance and run all migrations from v1 to current. Tradeoff: GRDB.swift over CoreData - GRDB gives SQL-level control and easier SQLite file portability for future iCloud sync via CloudKit CKAsset, but requires manual migration scripts. Tradeoff: PDFKit over a third-party PDF library - PDFKit is AppKit-native and sandbox-safe but lacks advanced typesetting; acceptable for heat sheet tabular layouts.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose GRDB.swift over CoreData for portable SQLite files enabling future iCloud sync via CKAsset upload. Selected PDFKit for PDF generation to avoid third-party dependencies incompatible with App Sandbox. Decided against iCloud sync in MVP to keep sandbox entitlements minimal. Sparkle update framework added in Phase 2 alongside the network entitlement already present for Sentry.

metadata.notes: macOS desktop app for elite swim coach race analysis; SwiftUI, GRDB.swift, PDFKit, Xcode Cloud CI/CD.

project_goal.value: AI system modernizing paper-based basketball scouting workflows: scouts dictate voice observations, which are transcribed and structured by an LLM into standardized player evaluation reports stored searchable in a vector database alongside historical scouting data
project_goal.rationale: Teams waste 60% of scout time on manual report transcription; LLM structuring enables semantic search across scouting history

target_users.value: NBA-level scouts, front-office analysts, and general managers at professional basketball organizations
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in scouting department discovery sessions

project_class.rationale: Core value is LLM structuring of voice input and semantic search over scouting history

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full AI system: voice ingestion, LLM structuring, vector search, dashboard, Cognito auth

complexity_level.rationale: Voice transcription pipeline, LLM structured output, vector similarity search, multi-role access control

risk_level.rationale: Confidential player evaluations under league NDA; LLM prompt logs require access controls

data_sensitivity.rationale: Player evaluation data is proprietary competitive intelligence subject to league NDAs

external_exposure.rationale: Accessible only to authenticated team personnel; no public or partner API

access_model.value: Cognito user pools with RBAC: scout (create/view own reports), analyst (read all reports, vector search), GM (draft board, aggregate views, approve), admin (user management)
access_model.rationale: Draft board and prospect evaluations must be restricted by role to prevent leaks

feature_scope.value: Voice note recording and transcription via AWS Transcribe, LLM structuring into evaluation schema, scout review and approval flow, vector embedding and semantic search over scouting history, prospect profile aggregation, draft board view with consensus scoring, LLM-authored GM summary
feature_scope.rationale: Complete scout-to-GM workflow replacing paper reports

mvp_scope.value: Voice transcription, LLM report structuring, scout approval, vector storage and semantic search
mvp_scope.rationale: Core AI workflow to validate with 3 pilot scouts before building draft board views

security_baseline.value: Cognito MFA for GM and admin roles, TLS 1.3, encryption at rest for all scouting data via KMS, LLM prompt logs access-controlled and subject to same retention policy as reports
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential competitive intelligence and league NDA obligations

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; React works well for report review forms and draft board drag-and-drop

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for LLM integrations (LangChain, OpenAI SDK); FastAPI async performance for concurrent voice upload and LLM calls

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for relational data; pgvector extension for report embeddings; Redis 7 for job status polling
data_platform.rationale: pgvector co-locates vector search with relational data avoiding an additional vector DB dependency

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Team AWS expertise; Fargate for containerized backend; us-east-1 for latency to NBA-market cities

privacy_retention_policy.value: Scouting reports retained indefinitely as competitive IP; voice transcriptions and LLM prompt logs retained for 2 years then purged; deletion on scout departure via admin API
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Reports are long-term competitive intelligence; raw voice and prompts are processing artifacts

future_scope.value: Trade value analyzer, draft combine data ingestion, multi-team license model
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 expansion after single-team validation

constraints.value: LLM must not train on team scouting data; OpenAI API used with data processing addendum; all prompt logs stored privately
constraints.rationale: League NDA prohibits sharing prospect evaluations with third parties including model training

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, structured JSON logging, PagerDuty on LLM error rate >5% or voice processing queue depth >20
observability_baseline.rationale: LLM and transcription pipelines have higher error variance than typical APIs; proactive alerting needed

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; 3 pilot scouts during MVP phase with weekly feedback sessions
execution_preference.rationale: Tight feedback loop with scouts essential for evaluation schema validation

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II required for enterprise basketball team clients; league NDA compliance for all prospect data; OpenAI data processing addendum signed
compliance_context.rationale: Confidential data sensitivity and medium risk require formal security attestation

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o for report structuring with JSON mode; text-embedding-3-large for vector embeddings; LangChain for prompt chain orchestration; system prompt locked to evaluation schema; OpenAI data processing addendum in place
llm_integration.rationale: GPT-4o JSON mode ensures structured output conformance; text-embedding-3-large for high-quality semantic similarity over scouting vocabulary

project.goal: AI system for basketball scout voice-to-structured-report workflow with semantic search
project.target_users: Scouts, analysts, GMs at professional basketball teams
project.timeline: MVP in 14 weeks, draft board in 20 weeks

frontend.approach: Scout Report form with approval flow, Semantic Search panel, Draft Board drag-and-drop view

backend.framework: FastAPI with LangChain and OpenAI SDK

security.auth: Cognito user pools with MFA for GM/admin, RBAC
security.encryption: KMS at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.cache: Redis 7 for voice processing job status

constraints.llm_training: OpenAI must not use team data for model training; DPA required
constraints.prompt_logs: LLM prompt logs private and access-controlled

reasoner_reviews.product: The voice-to-structured-report pipeline is the product's core differentiator. Scout approval before reports are indexed is essential for data quality - unapproved transcriptions should never enter the vector database or be visible to analysts. The draft board consensus view and GM summary are high-value features that depend entirely on report quality upstream.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: pgvector inside PostgreSQL is the correct choice for this scale - it avoids the operational overhead of a separate vector database and keeps transactional and semantic data consistent. The LLM structuring step should be async and jobs polled via Redis to prevent HTTP timeout on long audio files. LangChain prompt chain should version system prompts so changes to the evaluation schema do not silently break historical report consistency.
reasoner_reviews.security: LLM prompt logs containing player evaluations are as sensitive as the reports themselves - they must be stored in the same access-controlled table as reports and subject to the same 2-year retention policy. Cognito MFA for GM and admin roles is non-negotiable given draft board sensitivity. KMS encryption at rest must cover both PostgreSQL RDS and S3 voice file storage.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Voice recordings containing player evaluations are classified as proprietary scouting intelligence — they must be stored with AES-256 encryption and access restricted to the originating scout and authorised coaching staff. The Whisper transcription step must complete within 60 seconds of recording submission to maintain scout workflow pace. Single-region deployment in us-east-1 required; no cross-region data replication of player evaluation records to limit exposure surface.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with role-gated route groups: /scout/* (report creation, own report list), /analyst/* (search, all reports), /gm/* (draft board, aggregate views). React Query for server state; Zustand for draft board local drag state.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Voice Record page: browser MediaRecorder API records note, uploads to S3 presigned URL, polls job status via Redis-backed GET /reports/jobs/{jobId}. Report Review page: structured evaluation fields pre-filled by LLM, scout edits and approves. Semantic Search page: free-text query input, results with similarity score and prospect name. Draft Board page: draggable prospect cards in rank order, GM approve/lock action.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: React route guards check Cognito group membership; analyst routes reject scout role tokens. No scouting report content rendered until JWT validated and RBAC checked server-side.

backend.service_design: FastAPI with routers: voice_ingestion (S3 presigned URL, transcription job trigger), reports (LLM structuring, CRUD, approval), search (pgvector similarity query), draft_board (aggregate, GM summary generation), admin (user management).
backend.llm_integration_design: LangChain chain: TranscriptionResult -> StructuringChain (GPT-4o JSON mode, system prompt with PlayerEvaluationSchema) -> EmbeddingStep (text-embedding-3-large) -> PostgreSQL upsert. System prompt versioned in prompt_versions table. Async: Celery worker processes transcription to structuring pipeline; job status in Redis.
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat: nightly prompt_log_purge_job deletes prompt_logs older than 2 years. Weekly embedding_refresh_job re-embeds reports if the embedding model version changes.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with user groups: scouts, analysts, gm, admin. JWT claims include group; FastAPI dependency extracts role. MFA enforced for gm and admin groups via Cognito advanced security mode.
security.authorization_model: Report create: scout role only. Report read: scout sees own and approved; analyst sees all approved; GM sees all. Draft board write: GM only. LLM prompt log read: admin only.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Audit log written on every report approval, draft board change, and GM summary generation. Prompt logs stored in prompt_logs table with access restricted to admin role.

data.entities: scouts (id, cognito_sub, name, role), prospect_profiles (id, player_name, position, birth_year, current_team), scouting_reports (id, prospect_id, scout_id, voice_s3_key, transcription_text, structured_json, embedding vector(1536), approval_status ENUM pending/approved/rejected, approved_by, created_at), evaluation_dimensions (id, report_id, dimension_name, score_1_10, notes), prompt_logs (id, report_id, prompt_text, response_text, model_version, created_at), draft_boards (id, season_year, created_by_gm_id, locked_at), draft_board_entries (id, draft_board_id, prospect_id, rank_position, gm_notes)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS with KMS encryption; pgvector index type ivfflat on scouting_reports.embedding with lists=100; S3 bucket with KMS SSE for voice files; presigned URLs for scout upload with 15-minute expiry.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: API service (2-4 tasks), Celery worker service (1-3 tasks, CPU-based scaling). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in us-east-1. Redis ElastiCache for job status. S3 bucket in us-east-1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest with 80% coverage gate, ruff lint, bandit security scan, Docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy to staging, smoke tests, approval gate, production deploy. Database migrations via Alembic in pre-deploy task.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS deploy with minimum healthy percent 100% - old tasks kept until new tasks pass health checks. Alembic downgrade script prepared for each migration.

ISS-001.title: LLM prompt logs not access-controlled
ISS-001.detail: Previous plan stored prompt_logs in a general table readable by analyst role, violating confidentiality of raw player evaluation content in prompts

history.note: Identified by security reviewer - prompt_log table lacked row-level access controls

ISS-002.title: Vector embedding index not defined
ISS-002.detail: Previous plan mentioned pgvector but did not specify index type, lists parameter, or query planner settings needed for sub-100ms similarity search over a large report corpus

history.note: Architect reviewer flagged missing index specification

focus_issues.title: LLM prompt logs not access-controlled
focus_issues.detail: Prompt logs must be restricted to admin role only; analyst and scout roles must have no access to raw LLM prompt/response pairs

focus_issues.title: Vector embedding index not defined
focus_issues.detail: pgvector ivfflat or hnsw index with tuned lists/ef_construction parameters required for semantic search performance

latest_recommendations: Restrict prompt_logs table to admin role with PostgreSQL GRANT; add RLS policy denying access to scout and analyst roles
Specify pgvector index type ivfflat with lists=100 for the scouting_reports.embedding column and document query planner settings

previous_plan.title: Basketball Scout AI Voice Report System - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: AI system for professional basketball scouting using voice-to-LLM structuring pipeline, pgvector semantic search, and draft board views. Built on FastAPI, PostgreSQL 16, and React 18 on AWS ECS Fargate.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate with async Celery worker for voice transcription and LLM structuring pipeline. pgvector in PostgreSQL for semantic search. Cognito RBAC.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA for GM/admin. KMS at rest. TLS 1.3. Prompt logs stored in prompt_logs table - no access restriction specified in round 1.
previous_plan.data_model: scouting_reports with embedding vector(1536) column. prompt_logs table created without explicit access controls. pgvector extension enabled but no index defined.

target_output.thinking_summary: Revision round addressing two issues from round 1 audit: ISS-001 (prompt log access control) and ISS-002 (pgvector index specification). ISS-001 fix: PostgreSQL GRANT revokes analyst and scout access to prompt_logs; RLS policy enforces admin-only reads; FastAPI dependency rejects non-admin requests to /prompt-logs endpoints. ISS-002 fix: ivfflat index with lists=100 on scouting_reports.embedding; SET ivfflat.probes=10 for query-time recall tuning; documented in schema notes and observability section with a p99 search latency alert.

fix_report.action_taken: Added PostgreSQL GRANT/REVOKE statements in Alembic migration removing SELECT on prompt_logs from the app_analyst and app_scout roles; added RLS policy prompt_logs_admin_only enforcing current_user = 'app_admin'; FastAPI /prompt-logs router protected by require_admin dependency rejecting non-admin JWTs with 403
fix_report.expected_outcome: Analyst and scout roles receive 403 on any prompt_logs access; only admin role can retrieve raw LLM prompt/response pairs; confidentiality of unreviewed player evaluation content in prompts preserved

fix_report.action_taken: Added ivfflat index on scouting_reports.embedding with lists=100 in Alembic migration; documented SET ivfflat.probes=10 in query service; added p99 semantic search latency metric and 300ms alert threshold in observability section
fix_report.expected_outcome: Semantic search over 50k+ approved reports returns results in under 300ms p99; index rebuild procedure documented for embedding model version changes

target_output.title: Basketball Scout AI Voice Report System
target_output.executive_summary: An AI system modernizing professional basketball scouting by converting scout voice notes into structured LLM-generated player evaluation reports stored with vector embeddings for semantic search. Built on Python 3.12 FastAPI with Celery async workers, PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector, and React 18 on AWS ECS Fargate. Scouts record voice observations during games; AWS Transcribe and GPT-4o structure the transcript into a standardized evaluation schema. Approved reports are embedded and indexed for semantic similarity search by analysts and aggregated into draft board views for GMs. OpenAI data processing addendum ensures scouting data is not used for model training.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture on AWS ECS Fargate: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront, FastAPI REST API behind an ALB, and async Celery workers processing the voice-to-report pipeline. Scout voice notes upload to S3 via presigned URLs; Celery triggers an AWS Transcribe job, polls for completion, sends the transcript to GPT-4o via LangChain with the versioned PlayerEvaluationSchema system prompt, stores the structured JSON and generates a text-embedding-3-large vector, writes the report to PostgreSQL with pgvector ivfflat index for semantic search. Cognito RBAC gates role-specific route groups. Prompt logs are stored in a PostgreSQL table with RLS restricting access to admin role only. All data encrypted at rest via KMS; TLS 1.3 in transit.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Query, Zustand for draft board state, Tailwind CSS. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, LangChain 0.2, OpenAI SDK (GPT-4o, text-embedding-3-large), Celery 5 with Redis broker. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension, RDS Multi-AZ with KMS encryption. Cache/Broker: Redis 7 ElastiCache. Auth: AWS Cognito user pools with MFA for GM/admin groups. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, CloudFront, S3, AWS Transcribe, Alembic migrations, AWS Secrets Manager. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Voice Ingestion: scout requests presigned S3 URL, records voice note in browser, uploads directly to S3; backend triggers Transcribe job and returns jobId. LLM Structuring: Celery worker polls Transcribe, sends transcript to GPT-4o with versioned evaluation schema prompt, stores structured JSON and embedding in scouting_reports. Scout Approval: scout reviews pre-filled report fields, edits evaluation dimension scores and notes, approves; report embedding becomes searchable. Semantic Search: analyst enters free-text query such as 'athletic two-guard with defensive upside', backend embeds query with text-embedding-3-large, queries pgvector for cosine similarity top-20 results. Draft Board: GM drags prospect cards in rank order, locks board, triggers LLM-authored consensus summary from all approved reports for each top prospect. Prompt Log Audit: admin queries raw GPT-4o prompt/response pairs for compliance review.
target_output.system_components: React SPA (CloudFront + S3), ALB with WAF, ECS Fargate API service (FastAPI), ECS Fargate Celery worker service, PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (pgvector ivfflat index on scouting_reports.embedding lists=100), Redis ElastiCache (Celery broker and job status), S3 bucket with KMS SSE (voice files), AWS Transcribe (async speech-to-text), OpenAI API (GPT-4o, text-embedding-3-large), Cognito user pool with MFA policy, Datadog APM sidecar, AWS Secrets Manager, AWS KMS.
target_output.workflows: Voice-to-Report Pipeline: Scout records 90-second voice note describing a prospect's first step quickness and defensive positioning - browser uploads MP3 to S3 presigned URL. FastAPI creates scouting_report row with status=transcribing and jobId. Celery worker calls StartTranscriptionJob, polls GetTranscriptionJob every 10 seconds, retrieves transcript on completion. LangChain StructuringChain sends transcript with system prompt specifying PlayerEvaluationSchema (athleticism 1-10, defense 1-10, shooting 1-10, basketball_iq 1-10, notes free text) to GPT-4o JSON mode. Structured JSON parsed, validated against schema, stored in scouting_reports.structured_json. text-embedding-3-large embeds the full transcript and structured notes; vector stored in scouting_reports.embedding. Report status set to pending_review; scout notified via WebSocket push. Semantic Search: Analyst types 'high-motor center who protects the rim and runs the floor' - FastAPI embeds query with text-embedding-3-large, executes SELECT id, player_name from scouting_reports ORDER BY embedding <=> query_vector LIMIT 20 with SET ivfflat.probes=10; results returned with cosine similarity scores within 200ms for a corpus of 50k reports.
target_output.data_model: scouts (id UUID PK, cognito_sub TEXT, name TEXT, role ENUM scout/analyst/gm/admin). prospect_profiles (id UUID PK, player_name TEXT, position ENUM pg/sg/sf/pf/c, birth_year INT, current_team TEXT). scouting_reports (id UUID PK, prospect_id UUID FK, scout_id UUID FK, voice_s3_key TEXT, transcription_text TEXT, structured_json JSONB, embedding vector(1536), approval_status ENUM pending/approved/rejected, approved_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). ivfflat index on scouting_reports (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (lists=100). evaluation_dimensions (id UUID PK, report_id UUID FK, dimension_name TEXT, score_1_10 INT, notes TEXT). prompt_logs (id UUID PK, report_id UUID FK, prompt_text TEXT, response_text TEXT, model_version TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). REVOKE SELECT ON prompt_logs FROM app_analyst, app_scout. RLS POLICY prompt_logs_admin_only ON prompt_logs FOR SELECT USING (current_user = 'app_admin'). draft_boards (id UUID PK, season_year INT, created_by UUID, locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ). draft_board_entries (id UUID PK, draft_board_id UUID FK, prospect_id UUID FK, rank_position INT, gm_notes TEXT).
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. Voice ingestion: POST /api/v1/reports/voice-upload-url (returns presigned S3 URL and jobId), GET /api/v1/reports/jobs/{jobId} (poll status: transcribing/structuring/pending_review/failed). Reports: GET /api/v1/reports/{reportId}, PATCH /api/v1/reports/{reportId}/approve (scout role), GET /api/v1/reports?prospectId={id} (analyst and GM roles). Search: POST /api/v1/search/semantic (body: query string, returns list of reportId, playerName, similarity, excerpt). Draft board: GET /api/v1/draft-boards/{boardId}, PUT /api/v1/draft-boards/{boardId}/entries (GM role), POST /api/v1/draft-boards/{boardId}/gm-summary (GM role). Prompt logs: GET /api/v1/prompt-logs?reportId={id} (admin role only, 403 for all other roles).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pools: MFA enforced for gm and admin groups via Cognito advanced security mode. FastAPI RBAC dependency extracts Cognito group claim; route-level guards reject insufficient roles. prompt_logs: PostgreSQL GRANT/REVOKE removes SELECT from app_analyst and app_scout roles; RLS policy prompt_logs_admin_only enforces admin-only reads at DB layer; FastAPI /prompt-logs router decorated with require_admin dependency returning 403 for non-admin JWTs. KMS encryption at rest: RDS PostgreSQL encrypted with KMS CMK; S3 bucket SSE-KMS for voice files. TLS 1.3 for all connections. LLM data policy: OpenAI DPA in place; opt-out of training data use confirmed; API key stored in Secrets Manager, never logged. SOC 2 Type II: evidence collection for CC6 logical access, CC7 monitoring, CC9 risk management.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate: API service 2-4 tasks (HPA on CPU 65%), Celery worker service 1-3 tasks (HPA on Celery queue depth). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in us-east-1a/1c. ElastiCache Redis cluster mode disabled (single shard, 2 replicas). GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, bandit security scan, pytest with 80% coverage gate including integration tests against RDS-compatible TestContainers. Docker build and ECR push. ECS rolling deploy to staging, API smoke tests (voice upload URL, report approval, semantic search latency). Approval gate. Production ECS rolling deploy. Alembic pre-deploy migration task includes ivfflat index creation and prompt_logs REVOKE migration. Rollback: ECS service update to previous task definition within 5 minutes.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: distributed traces across FastAPI and Celery with prospect_id and scout_id tags. Custom metrics: voice_transcription_duration_seconds histogram, llm_structuring_duration_seconds histogram, semantic_search_p99_latency_ms (target under 300ms), celery_queue_depth, prompt_log_access_denied_count (security metric for auditing unexpected 403 spikes). PagerDuty alerts: LLM error rate over 5% (Transcribe or OpenAI failures), Celery queue depth over 20 for more than 5 minutes, semantic_search_p99_latency_ms over 300ms, RDS connection saturation over 80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: approximately $180/month for API and worker tasks at scout team scale of 50-100 daily reports during draft season. RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ: approximately $180/month. Redis cache.t3.micro: approximately $25/month. OpenAI API: approximately $0.15 per structured report (GPT-4o JSON mode and embedding). AWS Transcribe: $0.024/minute of audio. Total approximately $450/month at 100 reports/day. Scale trigger: ivfflat index rebuild required when embedding count exceeds 500k - schedule offline rebuild during off-season maintenance window.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (14 weeks): voice upload S3 presigned URL, AWS Transcribe async pipeline, GPT-4o LLM structuring with versioned system prompt, scout approval workflow, pgvector ivfflat index with lists=100, semantic search endpoint, Cognito RBAC with MFA for GM/admin, prompt_logs access controls addressing ISS-001, Alembic migrations, CI/CD pipeline, 3 pilot scouts. Phase 2 (6 weeks): draft board drag-and-drop UI, GM consensus summary LLM generation, prospect profile aggregation view, analyst bulk export, embedding refresh Celery job for model version changes. Phase 3 (6 weeks): SOC 2 Type II evidence collection, historical report import from legacy paper scan OCR pipeline, multi-team license model foundation.
target_output.development_guidelines: LangChain prompt templates versioned in prompt_versions table - never hardcode system prompts in application code. All Celery tasks idempotent: re-running a structuring job for an existing report_id overwrites structured_json and re-embeds without creating duplicates. FastAPI dependencies for role enforcement applied at router registration, not ad-hoc per route handler. Alembic migration for prompt_logs REVOKE and RLS policy must be reviewed by a security engineer before merge. pgvector index parameters (lists, probes) documented in DATABASE.md. pytest integration tests assert 403 response for analyst role accessing /prompt-logs.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o evaluation schema changes silently degrade report quality. Mitigation: system prompt versioned in DB; weekly diff check alerts when prompt_versions table diverges from expected schema. Risk: ivfflat index accuracy degrades as corpus grows without lists retuning. Mitigation: scheduled quarterly index rebuild with lists recalculation based on current row count using recommended formula lists = sqrt(row_count). Tradeoff: async voice pipeline (Celery and Transcribe) introduces 60-120 second latency from upload to report ready - scouts notified via WebSocket push; acceptable for post-game workflow. Tradeoff: pgvector ivfflat over hnsw - ivfflat uses less memory and rebuilds faster; chosen for rebuild performance during off-season data migrations. ISS-001 resolved via PostgreSQL GRANT/REVOKE plus RLS at DB layer, not application-layer filtering alone - defense-in-depth.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001: prompt_logs restricted at both PostgreSQL GRANT level and FastAPI route level - dual-layer protection confirmed. ISS-002: ivfflat with lists=100 chosen over hnsw for faster index rebuild when re-embedding on model version change; SET ivfflat.probes=10 documented as query-time setting. OpenAI DPA confirmed - model training opt-out applied at organization level in OpenAI dashboard.

metadata.notes: Revision round fixing ISS-001 prompt log access control and ISS-002 pgvector index specification for basketball scouting AI system.

project_goal.value: High-performance API service for a fantasy sports platform serving real-time player statistics, projected points, and roster availability to millions of concurrent users during live game windows, with pre-computed projection snapshots refreshed every 30 seconds and aggressive caching on read paths
project_goal.rationale: Fantasy sports engagement is highest during live games; sub-100ms API reads are the primary SLA driving retention

target_users.value: Fantasy sports platform developers integrating the API, and by extension millions of end users refreshing lineups during live game windows
target_users.rationale: B2C fantasy apps are the primary consumers; SLA targets are defined by user-perceived latency during peak game windows

project_class.rationale: Headless high-throughput API; no UI - data flows from ingestion pipeline through cache to downstream fantasy apps

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, realtime, analytics
capabilities.rationale: High-throughput REST API with real-time projection updates, JWT authentication, and analytics on consumption patterns

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-million concurrent users during NFL Sunday window; requires pre-computation, multi-layer caching, and adaptive rate limiting

risk_level.rationale: No financial or health data; risk from API abuse, SLA violations, and data tiering enforcement

data_sensitivity.rationale: Player statistics are public sports data; no PII or financial data stored

external_exposure.rationale: Fantasy app developers access the API over the public internet with JWT authentication

access_model.value: JWT API keys issued per fantasy app consumer with tier-based rate limits: free (100 req/min), standard (1000 req/min), premium (10000 req/min)
access_model.rationale: Tiered access model aligns rate limits with revenue tiers; adaptive throttling protects premium consumers during surges

feature_scope.value: Player statistics endpoint, projection snapshot endpoint updated every 30 seconds, roster availability endpoint, live game window detection, adaptive rate limiting by consumer tier, pre-computed snapshot pipeline, read replica routing for cache misses, analytics on consumer access patterns
feature_scope.rationale: Core data surfaces required by fantasy apps to support lineup decisions during live games

mvp_scope.value: Player statistics and projection snapshot endpoints with Redis caching, JWT authentication with tier-based rate limiting, 30-second projection refresh pipeline
mvp_scope.rationale: Core read path and refresh pipeline to validate latency targets before adding analytics

security_baseline.value: JWT API key authentication on every request, TLS 1.3, per-consumer rate limiting in Redis, WAF blocking malformed requests and crawler patterns, no PII stored
security_baseline.rationale: Public internet exposure requires rate limiting and WAF protection

frontend_stack.value: none - headless API service
frontend_stack.rationale: api_service project class with no UI component

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Fiber framework
backend_stack.rationale: Go Fiber achieves sub-millisecond handler overhead at high concurrency; critical for meeting 50ms p99 target on cached reads

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 read replicas for cache misses; Redis 7 Cluster for projection snapshot cache and rate limit counters; ClickHouse for consumer analytics
data_platform.rationale: Redis serves 95%+ of reads; PostgreSQL read replicas shield write-heavy ingestion; ClickHouse handles analytical queries without impacting transactional DB

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with ALB in us-east-1, multi-AZ
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate scales task count during NFL Sunday without pre-provisioning; ALB distributes across AZs

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data retained; API access logs retained 90 days for abuse investigation; projection snapshot history retained 7 days for replay
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No PII data retention obligations; access logs for security purposes only

future_scope.value: WebSocket subscription endpoint for live score push, GraphQL API variant, player prop odds enrichment
future_scope.rationale: WebSocket push will reduce polling load after core API is stable

constraints.value: p99 latency under 50ms for cached reads, under 200ms for cache miss reads; projection snapshots must be fully refreshed within 30-second window; cost target under $15k/month at peak NFL Sunday load
constraints.rationale: 50ms SLA is table stakes for fantasy app retention; 30-second projection staleness is the fantasy market expectation

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with per-endpoint p50/p95/p99 latency, Redis hit rate, cache miss rate, projection refresh lag, and per-consumer tier request volume; PagerDuty on p99 greater than 50ms sustained 3 minutes or Redis hit rate below 90%
observability_baseline.rationale: Cache hit rate and projection refresh lag are the two leading indicators of SLA risk

execution_preference.value: Load-test validated before each NFL season opener; blue-green deploy for zero downtime during game windows
execution_preference.rationale: Any deployment-related downtime during an NFL window causes immediate customer churn

project.goal: High-throughput fantasy sports stats API with 30-second projection refresh and Redis caching
project.target_users: Fantasy sports app developers and millions of end users
project.timeline: NFL season opener load-test target in 10 weeks

backend.api: REST with OpenAPI 3.1; WebSocket in future scope

security.auth: JWT API key per consumer
security.rate_limiting: Per-tier Redis counters with sliding window

devops.hosting: AWS ECS Fargate and ALB multi-AZ

constraints.latency: p99 under 50ms cached reads, under 200ms cache miss

reasoner_reviews.product: The 30-second projection refresh SLA and sub-50ms read latency are non-negotiable differentiators for fantasy apps. The adaptive rate limiter protecting premium consumers during surge is a key retention feature - free-tier consumers hammering endpoints during NFL Sunday must not degrade premium response times.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The read path design is correct: Redis Cluster for hot keys, read replica fallback for misses. The projection refresh pipeline needs careful design to ensure all active player snapshots are written within the 30-second window even under NFL Sunday write load. Pipelined Redis MSET is the right approach for bulk snapshot writes. The Fiber handler should resolve consumer tier from a cached JWT claims lookup to avoid a Redis round-trip on every request for rate limit tier detection.
reasoner_reviews.security: Per-consumer rate limiting with Redis sliding window counters is the right approach. WAF rules should target scraper patterns (high request frequency with no cache-hit patterns) in addition to standard injection protection. JWT rotation policy for compromised API keys must be documented.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The sub-50ms read latency SLA applies at the 99th percentile under sustained load of 50,000 concurrent fantasy app users during Sunday NFL game windows. Redis Cluster must have at least 3 shards to distribute hot-key load across game-day peak traffic. The projection refresh pipeline must not block the read path — stale projections are preferable to elevated read latency. Single AWS region deployment in us-east-1 for proximity to primary sports data feed providers.

backend.service_design: Go Fiber application with handler packages: stats (player statistics retrieval), projections (snapshot read, pre-computation trigger), availability (roster status), auth (JWT middleware, tier extraction), ratelimit (Redis sliding window middleware).
backend.api_patterns: GET /v1/players/{playerId}/stats?season={year}&week={week}. GET /v1/projections/snapshot?gameId={id} returns full pre-computed snapshot for all active players in a game. GET /v1/players/{playerId}/availability. GET /v1/games/live returns IDs of currently active game windows. All endpoints accept If-None-Match for conditional GET support.
backend.scaling_notes: Redis pipeline for 30-second snapshot write: single MSET across all active player projection keys with TTL=35s. Fargate HPA target CPU 60% with minimum 4 tasks, maximum 40 tasks. ALB connection draining 30s for blue-green deploy.
backend.failure_handling: Redis circuit breaker: if Redis Cluster latency greater than 20ms for 10 consecutive requests, fall through to read replica with 200ms timeout. Projection snapshot staleness detection: if last_refresh_at greater than 35 seconds ago, response includes X-Projection-Stale: true header.

security.auth_design: JWT API key verified with HMAC-SHA256; claims include consumerId, tier (free/standard/premium), and expiresAt. Key rotation: admin API issues new key, old key grace period 24 hours. Compromised key revocation: revoked consumer IDs stored in Redis Set checked on every request.
security.abuse_prevention: Redis sliding window rate limiter: INCR with EXPIRE per consumerId per minute window; free tier 100/min, standard 1000/min, premium 10000/min. Adaptive throttle: if total ECS CPU exceeds 80%, dynamically halve free-tier limit. WAF rules: block IPs with more than 10k requests per minute and no cache-hit correlation.

data.entities: players (id, name, position, team_id, status ENUM active/injured/suspended), player_statistics (id, player_id, game_id, season_year, week, completions, rushing_yards, receiving_yards, touchdowns, fantasy_points_ppr), projection_snapshots (id, player_id, game_id, projected_points_ppr, projected_carries, projected_targets, confidence_score, computed_at), game_windows (id, sport, home_team_id, away_team_id, kickoff_utc, status ENUM scheduled/live/final), cache_manifests (id, game_id, snapshot_key_prefix, last_refresh_at, player_count)
data.storage_design: Redis 7 Cluster: projection snapshot keys proj:{gameId}:{playerId} with TTL 35s; rate limit keys rl:{consumerId}:{windowMinute}; revoked key set revoked_consumers. PostgreSQL 16: primary for writes (ingestion pipeline), 2 read replicas for cache miss fallback. ClickHouse: player_statistics_events table for analytical queries.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: API service cluster with ALB in us-east-1a and us-east-1b. Redis 7 Cluster 3-node with replicas per shard. PostgreSQL 16 primary and 2 read replicas. ClickHouse 2-node cluster.
devops.ci_cd_design: Buildkite: go test -race ./..., golangci-lint, k6 load test against staging (target 50k RPS, p99 under 50ms), Docker push to ECR, ECS blue-green deploy with ALB target group weight shift from 0% to 100% over 2 minutes. Rollback: shift ALB weight back to old target group within 90 seconds.
devops.cost_controls: Fargate Spot for non-NFL-window hours (scale to 4 tasks on Spot). Reserved ECS capacity for 8-task baseline during NFL season. Redis cluster sized for 32GB hot data; eviction policy allkeys-lru. ClickHouse aggregations pre-materialized to reduce query cost.

target_output.title: Fantasy Sports Real-Time Statistics API
target_output.executive_summary: A high-throughput API service powering real-time fantasy sports lineup decisions for millions of concurrent users during NFL game windows. Built on Go 1.22 with Fiber, Redis 7 Cluster for projection snapshot caching, and PostgreSQL 16 read replicas for cache miss fallback on AWS ECS Fargate. A dedicated projection engine service with a 4-goroutine worker pool refreshes all active player snapshots via Redis MSET pipeline every 30 seconds, monitored by a refresh_lag_seconds metric. Tiered JWT rate limiting with adaptive throttling protects premium consumers during free-tier surges. Redis circuit breaker automatically falls back to read replicas on cluster degradation.
target_output.architecture_overview: The read path serves over 95% of requests from Redis 7 Cluster: Go Fiber handlers decode the JWT, check the consumer tier from a cached claims lookup, apply Redis sliding window rate limit, and serve the projection snapshot from Redis key proj:{gameId}:{playerId} with a TTL of 35 seconds. Cache misses fall through to PostgreSQL read replicas via a connection pool, guarded by a circuit breaker that detects Redis degradation and reroutes reads to the replica tier for a 30-second backoff window before reattempting Redis. A separate projection engine ECS service with a 4-goroutine worker pool runs continuously: each worker claims an active game window, fetches the latest play-by-play events from the ingestion database, recomputes projected points for all active players in that game, and writes the full player set to Redis via a pipelined MSET of up to 500 keys per batch. The refresh cycle timer publishes a refresh_lag_seconds metric to Datadog; if any game window exceeds 25 seconds, PagerDuty fires. ClickHouse receives a stream of all statistical events for analytical queries served via a separate read path isolated from the core stats endpoints.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Fiber v2, go-redis v9 ClusterClient. Cache: Redis 7 Cluster (3 shards, 1 replica per shard). Database: PostgreSQL 16 primary and 2 read replicas with PgBouncer connection pool. Analytics: ClickHouse 24.x with pre-materialized aggregates. Auth: HMAC-SHA256 JWT API keys. Rate limiting: Redis sliding window counters. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (API service and projection engine service), ALB multi-AZ, ECR, AWS Secrets Manager. CI/CD: Buildkite with k6 load tests. Observability: Datadog APM with custom metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Player Statistics Endpoint: GET /v1/players/{playerId}/stats serves historical and current season stats from PostgreSQL read replica; cache miss allowed given stats are not real-time. Projection Snapshot Endpoint: GET /v1/projections/snapshot?gameId={id} serves pre-computed snapshot from Redis; responds with X-Projection-Stale:true header if last_refresh_at greater than 35 seconds. Roster Availability: GET /v1/players/{playerId}/availability serves injury and game-time status with 60-second Redis TTL. Live Game Windows: GET /v1/games/live returns IDs and kickoff times of currently active game windows for client polling orchestration. Projection Refresh Pipeline: continuously running projection engine service recomputes and caches all active player projections on 30-second cycle. Adaptive Rate Limiting: free-tier limits halved when ECS cluster CPU exceeds 80%; premium consumers maintain full quota.
target_output.system_components: Go Fiber API service (ECS Fargate, 4-40 tasks HPA CPU 60%). Redis middleware (ClusterClient with RouteRandomly:true, circuit breaker at 10 consecutive ops exceeding 20ms p99). JWT middleware (HMAC-SHA256 verify, claims cache in sync.Map, revoked consumer check in Redis Set). Rate limit middleware (Redis sliding window INCR/EXPIRE per consumerId per minute, adaptive threshold on cluster CPU). Projection Engine service (ECS Fargate, 4-8 tasks, 4-goroutine worker pool per task, game window queue in Redis List, refresh cycle timer, MSET pipeline batch 500 keys). Redis 7 Cluster (3 shards, 1 replica each, 32GB total, allkeys-lru eviction). PostgreSQL 16 primary and 2 read replicas via PgBouncer. ClickHouse 2-node cluster. ALB with WAF. Datadog APM sidecar. AWS Secrets Manager for JWT signing key.
target_output.workflows: Projection Refresh Cycle: projection engine worker dequeues active game window ID from Redis List game_window_queue. Worker queries PostgreSQL read replica for all player_statistics rows and ingestion pipeline events for that gameId in the last 5 minutes. Projection computation: each active player's projected_points_ppr recalculated from current snap count, targets, and Vegas implied totals. MSET pipeline built with keys proj:{gameId}:{p1}, proj:{gameId}:{p2} up to 500 entries per pipeline flush, TTL 35s. Cycle timer records elapsed seconds; after all game windows processed in current 30-second window, publishes refresh_lag_seconds to Datadog. API Read with Redis Circuit Breaker: consumer requests GET /v1/projections/snapshot?gameId=NFL_2026_W5_PHI_DAL. JWT middleware decodes token, checks consumerId in revoked_consumers Redis Set. Rate limit middleware increments rl:{consumerId}:{windowMinute} - if over tier limit returns 429. Redis GET proj:NFL_2026_W5_PHI_DAL:{playerId} for each requested player. Cache hit: return snapshot in under 5ms. Cache miss or circuit open: query PostgreSQL read replica, return snapshot with X-Projection-Stale:true if last_refresh_at exceeds 35s. Circuit breaker records Redis latency; if 10 consecutive operations exceed 20ms, sets circuit_open=true in process memory, routes all reads to replica for 30 seconds, publishes redis_circuit_open=1 metric.
target_output.data_model: players (id UUID PK, name TEXT, position ENUM qb/rb/wr/te/k/def, team_id UUID, status ENUM active/injured/suspended, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). player_statistics (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, game_id UUID FK, season_year INT, week INT, completions INT, rushing_yards INT, receiving_yards INT, touchdowns INT, fantasy_points_ppr NUMERIC). projection_snapshots (id UUID PK, player_id UUID FK, game_id UUID FK, projected_points_ppr NUMERIC, projected_carries INT, projected_targets INT, confidence_score NUMERIC 0.0-1.0, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). game_windows (id UUID PK, sport TEXT, home_team_id UUID, away_team_id UUID, kickoff_utc TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM scheduled/live/final). cache_manifests (id UUID PK, game_id UUID FK, last_refresh_at TIMESTAMPTZ, player_count INT, refresh_lag_seconds NUMERIC). Redis: proj:{gameId}:{playerId} STRING (JSON projection snapshot) TTL 35s; rl:{consumerId}:{windowMinute} INCR EXPIRE; revoked_consumers SET; game_window_queue LIST.
target_output.api_design: REST at /v1. GET /v1/players/{playerId}/stats?season={year}&week={week} (supports If-None-Match ETag, returns player_statistics JSON). GET /v1/projections/snapshot?gameId={id} (returns all active player projections for game, Redis-cached; header X-Projection-Stale:true if stale). GET /v1/players/{playerId}/availability (Redis-cached 60s; returns status, injury designation, game_time_decision_flag). GET /v1/games/live (returns list of gameId, sport, homeTeam, awayTeam, kickoffUTC for currently live windows). All endpoints: Authorization: Bearer {jwtApiKey}. Rate limit headers X-RateLimit-Remaining and X-RateLimit-Reset on every response. 429 response with Retry-After header on exhaustion. Analytics: GET /v1/analytics/consumption?consumerId={id}&from={ts} (admin scope, served from ClickHouse).
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT API key authentication: HMAC-SHA256 signed, claims include consumerId, tier, expiresAt. Signing key in AWS Secrets Manager, rotated quarterly. Revoked consumer IDs in Redis Set revoked_consumers checked on every request, updated within 1 second of revocation. WAF rules: ALB WAF blocks IPs with more than 10k req/min and bot-pattern User-Agents, rate limits per CIDR. TLS 1.3 enforced at ALB. No PII stored - no GDPR or CCPA data subject obligations for player stats data. API key rotation: compromised key revoked via admin endpoint, 24-hour grace period for old key, new key issued immediately. Adaptive throttle: per-tier limits enforced in Redis middleware; dynamic limit halving for free tier on cluster CPU alert.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate API service: 4-40 tasks, HPA CPU target 60%. ECS Fargate projection engine service: 4-8 tasks (scale on game_window_queue depth). ALB target group blue-green: new task group receives 0% weight, health check passes, weight shifted 10% every 30 seconds to 100% over approximately 5 minutes; old task group drained with 30-second connection draining. Rollback: ALB weight shifted back to old target group within 90 seconds if error rate spikes. Buildkite pipeline: golangci-lint, go test -race ./..., k6 load test at 50k RPS with p99 latency assertion under 50ms on staging, Docker ECR push, ECS blue-green deploy script. Redis 7 Cluster provisioned via Terraform with 3 shards and 1 replica each; automatic failover enabled. PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ RDS with 2 read replicas; PgBouncer pool size tuned for 40 API tasks plus 8 projection engine tasks. NFL Sunday scale-up: ECS service desired count pre-warmed to 20 API tasks and 6 projection engine tasks 30 minutes before kickoff via scheduled EventBridge rule.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: distributed traces across API handler, Redis middleware, and PostgreSQL fallback with gameId and consumerId tags. Custom metrics: redis_hit_rate (target above 95%), refresh_lag_seconds per gameId (target under 25s), redis_circuit_open (binary alert), cache_miss_fallback_rate, p50/p95/p99 latency per endpoint, rate_limit_429_rate per consumer tier. PagerDuty alerts: p99 latency exceeds 50ms sustained 3 minutes, redis_hit_rate below 90% sustained 2 minutes, refresh_lag_seconds exceeds 25 for any active game window, redis_circuit_open=1, ECS task count below 4 during live game window. Datadog dashboard: NFL Sunday game window panel showing refresh lag per game, Redis hit rate trending, projection engine worker utilization, top consumers by request volume.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate API service: approximately $800/month baseline (4 tasks), approximately $4000/month NFL Sunday peak (20 tasks on Fargate Spot). Projection engine: approximately $200/month 4 tasks. Redis Cluster 3-shard r6g.large: approximately $600/month. RDS PostgreSQL db.r6g.large primary and 2 read replicas: approximately $1200/month. ClickHouse 2-node: approximately $400/month. ALB and WAF: approximately $300/month. Total estimated $8000/month at peak, under $15k/month constraint. Cost controls: Fargate Spot for non-game-window hours; EventBridge scheduled scale-up 30 minutes before kickoff prevents cold-start surge; Redis allkeys-lru eviction prevents OOM; ClickHouse pre-materialized aggregates reduce query compute.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Go Fiber API service with Redis-first read path, JWT authentication with tier-based rate limiting, projection snapshot and player stats endpoints, projection engine with 4-goroutine worker pool and MSET pipeline (ISS-001 fix), Redis circuit breaker with read replica fallback (ISS-002 fix), Buildkite CI with k6 load tests, ECS blue-green deploy, Datadog custom metrics including refresh_lag_seconds and redis_circuit_open, NFL season opener load test. Phase 2 (4 weeks): ClickHouse analytics integration, /analytics/consumption endpoint, consumer dashboard, adaptive throttle on cluster CPU. Phase 3 (4 weeks): WebSocket subscription endpoint for live score push, GraphQL API variant exploration, player prop odds enrichment endpoint.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go Fiber handlers must complete in under 1ms excluding I/O - profiled with pprof before each release. Redis operations never performed inside a goroutine without context timeout of 10ms. Projection engine MSET batches built as slices, never string concatenation. All ECS task definitions pin the Docker image SHA - no latest tag in production. k6 load test scripts stored alongside API code and run in Buildkite on every PR targeting main. Circuit breaker state must be process-local to avoid Redis dependency during Redis outage - sync.Mutex protected boolean in each API task.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: NFL Sunday concurrent write load from projection engine saturates Redis Cluster write capacity. Mitigation: MSET pipeline batching, projection engine horizontal scaling via game_window_queue depth HPA, and pre-warmed capacity 30 minutes before kickoff. ISS-001 resolved by 4-goroutine pool, MSET batching, and refresh_lag_seconds alerting at 25s buffer. Risk: Redis partial shard failure causes inconsistent snapshot availability for some gameIds. Mitigation: circuit breaker per-process detects latency degradation and routes all reads to replica tier within 200ms. ISS-002 resolved by go-redis ClusterClient with RouteRandomly:true and documented circuit breaker thresholds. Tradeoff: process-local circuit breaker state over distributed state - avoids Redis dependency during Redis outage; acceptable because each task makes independent fallback decisions with no coordination requirement.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001: projection engine 4-goroutine worker pool with MSET pipeline batch size 500 and refresh_lag_seconds Datadog metric with PagerDuty at 25 seconds. ISS-002: go-redis ClusterClient RouteRandomly:true; circuit breaker triggers at 10 consecutive Redis ops exceeding 20ms p99; 30-second backoff before retrying Redis; process-local state chosen over distributed to avoid recursive Redis dependency. Pre-warmed NFL Sunday capacity via EventBridge scheduled rule 30 minutes before kickoff.

metadata.notes: Revision round fixing projection refresh pipeline timing guarantees (ISS-001) and Redis Cluster failover behavior (ISS-002) for high-scale fantasy sports stats API.

project.goal: Publish youth soccer academy programs, coaches, and tryout info as a fast static site
project.target_users: Parents and youth players researching enrollment

security.headers: CSP blocking third-party scripts except Plausible

data.cms: Contentful for programs, coaches, tryout_events, announcements, gallery_items

devops.rebuild_trigger: Contentful webhook to Vercel Deploy Hook

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope is correctly minimized to informational content. The decision to exclude registration forms from the static site and defer to a separate backend service is the right call for COPPA compliance. SEO-optimized program pages with structured data markup will improve organic discovery by parents searching for local youth soccer academies.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 static export with Contentful is the standard pattern for this use case. The Vercel webhook-triggered rebuild means content updates go live within minutes of CMS saves with no engineering involvement. Recommend generateStaticParams for all tryout event pages to ensure pre-rendering rather than dynamic generation.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP header must block all script-src except the Plausible Analytics script domain. No form inputs means no CSRF surface. Confirm Plausible is configured in cookieless mode to satisfy COPPA without a consent banner.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Six-week timeline is achievable: Contentful schema setup week 1, Next.js component library and page templates weeks 2-3, content population by marketing weeks 4-5, launch week 6.

backend.service_design: No persistent backend. Single Vercel serverless function api/revalidate.ts receives Contentful webhook POST, validates webhook secret, and calls res.revalidate() for affected page paths.
backend.api_patterns: Contentful Content Delivery API GraphQL called at build time via getStaticProps. Webhook receiver: POST /api/revalidate with x-contentful-signature header validation.
backend.backend_risks: Contentful API rate limits during build — mitigated by incremental static regeneration for tryout event pages rather than full site rebuild on every content change.

security.auth_design: No user authentication. Contentful webhook receiver validates HMAC signature from x-contentful-signature header to prevent unauthorized rebuild triggers.
security.abuse_prevention: CSP header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' plausible.io; img-src 'self' images.ctfassets.net. No inline scripts permitted.
security.privacy_controls: Plausible Analytics in cookieless mode — no cookies set, GDPR and COPPA compliant.

data.entities: programs (id, name, age_group, season, description, schedule_summary, image), coaches (id, name, role, bio, photo_url, certifications[]), tryout_events (id, age_group, date, location, registration_deadline, notes), announcements (id, title, body, published_at, expires_at), gallery_items (id, image_url, caption, album_tag)
data.schema_notes: All entities modeled as Contentful content types. Build-time GraphQL queries fetch and transform to typed TypeScript interfaces. No runtime database.
data.data_risks: Contentful outage blocks site rebuilds — existing static pages remain live from CDN cache; only new content is delayed.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network serving static assets. GitHub main branch triggers Vercel deployment. Contentful publish events call Vercel Deploy Hook URL stored as Contentful webhook.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint, TypeScript tsc --noEmit, Lighthouse CI (performance score >90, accessibility >95) on every PR. Vercel preview deployments on every PR branch for marketing review.
devops.observability_stack: Plausible Analytics on all pages (cookieless). Vercel deployment log alerts via Slack webhook on build failure. Lighthouse CI score regressions block PR merge.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export with TypeScript; program listing and coaching staff pages generated at build time; event calendar as client island
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home, Program Listings, Coaching Staff Directory, Tryout Event Calendar, Facilities Gallery, Enrollment CTA
frontend.ui_modules: ProgramListingCard, CoachProfileCard, TryoutEventCalendar (client island), FacilitiesGallery, EnrollmentCTAButton
