previous_plan.title: Corporate Wellness App Architecture v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Flutter cross-platform wellness app with four Node.js Fastify microservices on GCP Cloud Run, MongoDB Atlas for activity and challenge data, Redis sorted sets for real-time leaderboards.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Four microservices behind Cloud Endpoints API gateway; GCP Pub/Sub for ActivityLogged, ChallengeGoalMet, PointsAwarded, MilestoneReached events; Firebase Auth JWT for authentication.
previous_plan.workflows: Activity log -> ActivityLogged event -> challenge-service updates leaderboard -> ChallengeGoalMet event -> rewards-service credits points -> notification-service sends FCM push.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: GDPR erasure via EmployeeDataDeletionRequested event (partial spec, no failure handling). HR admin aggregate analytics (no server-side minimum group size enforcement).

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must close the two GDPR compliance gaps. For ISS-001, design the complete erasure saga: erasure-coordinator-service receives DELETE request, creates erasure_requests document, publishes EmployeeDataDeletionRequested, each of the four services deletes records and publishes DeletionConfirmed, coordinator marks complete — with a dead letter queue for failed confirmations and a 30-day SLA monitor. For ISS-002, add server-side minimum group size enforcement in the challenge-service leaderboard endpoint and HR admin analytics endpoint — if the participant count is below 5, return a suppressed aggregate response rather than individual-level data.

fix_report.action_taken: Designed full GDPR erasure saga: employee submits DELETE /employees/me/data; erasure-coordinator-service creates erasure_requests document (status: pending, services_pending: [activity,challenge,rewards,notification]); publishes EmployeeDataDeletionRequested to Pub/Sub; each service hard-deletes records and publishes DeletionConfirmed to erasure-responses topic; coordinator updates services_pending list; marks erasure complete when all four confirm. Failed confirmations after 24h retry window go to erasure-dlq topic triggering PagerDuty alert. Nightly job checks erasure_requests where created_at < 29 days and status != complete and alerts compliance team.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure fulfilled within 30 days with full audit trail and failure alerting

fix_report.action_taken: Added server-side minimum group size enforcement: challenge-service leaderboard endpoint counts ZCARD on the leaderboard sorted set before returning data; if count < 5 returns {suppressed: true, reason: 'group_too_small', minimum_size: 5} with HTTP 200. HR admin analytics endpoints in challenge-service and rewards-service apply $group aggregation with $cond to suppress per-employee breakdown when challenge_participant count < 5, returning only total program-level aggregate.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No individual employee data indirectly identifiable via HR admin analytics even for small teams

target_output.title: Corporate Wellness Mobile App — Final Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Flutter cross-platform wellness app for corporate employees to log movement activity, compete in team step-count challenges, and earn redeemable reward points, backed by four Node.js Fastify microservices on GCP Cloud Run communicating via GCP Pub/Sub events. MongoDB Atlas stores employee activity, challenge, and rewards data with Redis sorted sets for real-time leaderboards. GDPR right-to-erasure is implemented via a choreographed saga with full failure handling and 30-day SLA enforcement. HR admin analytics enforce server-side minimum group size of 5 to prevent indirect employee identification.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four independently deployed Cloud Run services behind a Cloud Endpoints API gateway: activity-service (activity logging and wearable sync), challenge-service (team challenges, Redis leaderboard management), rewards-service (points accrual, redemption catalog), notification-service (FCM push delivery). An erasure-coordinator-service manages GDPR deletion sagas. Services communicate asynchronously via GCP Pub/Sub topics. Firebase Auth provides JWT authentication with RBAC custom claims. Flutter app uses drift SQLite offline queue for activity logging during poor connectivity, syncing on reconnection. MongoDB Atlas M30 per environment in us-central1.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.19 with Dart 3, Riverpod 2.0 state management, drift 2.x SQLite ORM for offline queue, Firebase Auth, Firebase Crashlytics. Backend: Node.js 22 with Fastify 4 (four microservices + erasure coordinator). Database: MongoDB 7 Atlas M30. Cache: GCP Memorystore Redis 7. Messaging: GCP Pub/Sub. API Gateway: Cloud Endpoints with JWT validation. CI/CD: CircleCI. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus, Firebase Crashlytics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Activity Logging: manual entry (activity type, duration, step count) + Apple Health/Google Fit sync via platform APIs; offline-first with drift SQLite sync queue. Team Challenges: HR admin creates challenge with step/minute/session goal, eligible employee IDs, and date range; employees see live leaderboard updated via Redis ZADD on ActivityLogged event. Reward Points: challenge-service publishes ChallengeGoalMet; rewards-service credits points; employee views balance and redemption catalog. Push Notifications: FCM notifications for milestone achievements, goal completions, and challenge deadlines via notification-service. HR Admin Analytics: program participation rates, aggregate step counts — all suppressed if group size < 5. GDPR Erasure: employee self-service deletion saga with 30-day SLA.
target_output.system_components: activity-service (Cloud Run, Fastify, MongoDB activity_logs collection, publishes ActivityLogged). challenge-service (Cloud Run, Fastify, MongoDB team_challenges + challenge_participants, Redis ZADD/ZRANK for leaderboards, subscribes ActivityLogged, publishes ChallengeGoalMet). rewards-service (Cloud Run, Fastify, MongoDB reward_points + redemption_events, subscribes ChallengeGoalMet, publishes PointsAwarded). notification-service (Cloud Run, Fastify, FCM, subscribes PointsAwarded + MilestoneReached). erasure-coordinator-service (Cloud Run, Fastify, MongoDB erasure_requests, orchestrates GDPR saga). Cloud Endpoints API gateway (JWT validation, RBAC claim routing). GCP Pub/Sub topics: ActivityLogged, ChallengeGoalMet, PointsAwarded, MilestoneReached, EmployeeDataDeletionRequested, DeletionConfirmed, erasure-dlq.
target_output.workflows: Activity Logging (online): Employee taps 'Log Activity'; POST /activities to activity-service; stored in MongoDB activity_logs; ActivityLogged event published to Pub/Sub. Activity Logging (offline): drift SQLite queue stores pending log; on reconnection Flutter sync manager replays queue via POST /activities/batch; activity-service deduplicates by (employee_id, logged_at) before inserting. Challenge Leaderboard Update: challenge-service consumes ActivityLogged; updates challenge_participants.current_progress; ZADD challenge:{id}:leaderboard employee_id new_score; if goal_value met publishes ChallengeGoalMet. GDPR Erasure Saga: employee submits DELETE /employees/me/data; erasure-coordinator creates erasure_requests doc (id, employee_id, status:pending, services_pending:[activity,challenge,rewards,notification], created_at); publishes EmployeeDataDeletionRequested; each service deletes employee records and publishes DeletionConfirmed(service_name); coordinator removes service_name from services_pending; when services_pending is empty marks status:complete. Failure path: services that fail to confirm within 24h are retried via erasure-dlq; if unresolved after 72h PagerDuty alert fires to compliance team.
target_output.data_model: employee_profiles (MongoDB: {_id, firebase_uid, corporate_email, company_id, created_at, consent_given_at, consent_version, employment_end_date}). activity_logs (MongoDB: {_id, employee_id, activity_type TEXT, duration_minutes INT, step_count INT, source ENUM('manual','apple_health','google_fit'), logged_at DATE, synced_at DATE}). team_challenges (MongoDB: {_id, company_id, name, goal_type ENUM('steps','minutes','sessions'), goal_value INT, start_date, end_date, eligible_employee_ids ARRAY, status ENUM('draft','active','completed')}). challenge_participants (MongoDB: {_id, challenge_id, employee_id, current_progress INT, goal_met_at DATE|null}). reward_points (MongoDB: {_id, employee_id, balance INT, last_updated}). redemption_events (MongoDB: {_id, employee_id, reward_catalog_id, points_cost INT, redeemed_at, status ENUM('pending','fulfilled','cancelled')}). erasure_requests (MongoDB: {_id, employee_id, status ENUM('pending','complete','failed'), services_pending ARRAY, created_at, completed_at}). Redis keys: challenge:{challenge_id}:leaderboard (sorted set, score=step_count, member=employee_id).
target_output.api_design: activity-service: POST /activities (log manual activity), POST /activities/batch (offline sync replay with dedup), GET /activities/me?since&until. challenge-service: GET /challenges?company_id (list active challenges), GET /challenges/{id}/leaderboard (returns {suppressed:true} if ZCARD < 5), GET /challenges/{id}/leaderboard/me (own rank only). rewards-service: GET /rewards/balance, GET /rewards/catalog, POST /rewards/redeem/{catalog_id}. HR admin: GET /admin/programs/{company_id}/analytics (aggregate only, minimum group 5 enforced), GET /admin/challenges/{id}/summary. erasure-coordinator: DELETE /employees/me/data (creates saga), GET /admin/erasure/{request_id}/status.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT validated at Cloud Endpoints; custom claims: role (employee|hr_admin), company_id. Employee role: activity endpoints scoped to own firebase_uid; challenge leaderboard returns team aggregate only, individual rank for self. HR admin role: blocked from all individual activity endpoints at gateway level; analytics endpoints enforce minimum group size 5 server-side — ZCARD < 5 returns suppressed response; $group aggregation suppresses per-employee fields when count < 5. GDPR: consent captured at onboarding with version tracking; DELETE /employees/me/data triggers erasure saga; erasure_requests collection tracks 30-day SLA; nightly SLA monitor alerts on pending requests approaching deadline. Data minimization: step_count only stored, not raw GPS; wearable sync uses read-only HealthKit/Google Fit scopes.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: CircleCI pipelines per service: ESLint, tsc type check, Jest 90% coverage, MongoDB testcontainer integration tests, Docker build, push to GCP Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy with 10% canary traffic split. Environments: dev (single Cloud Run revision), staging (mirrored production), production (multi-region us-central1 + us-east1 for enterprise clients). MongoDB Atlas global clusters for enterprise multi-region clients. Feature flags via GCP Feature Flags for per-corporate-client gradual rollout. Secrets managed via GCP Secret Manager.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics per service exposed on /metrics: activity ingestion rate (activities/s), challenge leaderboard update latency (p50/p99), FCM delivery success rate, erasure saga completion time. Grafana dashboards per service. Firebase Crashlytics for Flutter crash reporting with non-PII metadata only. Structured JSON logs with correlation_id and employee_id (hashed) for tracing. PagerDuty alerts: erasure saga failure (erasure-dlq messages), leaderboard Redis error, Cloud Run error rate > 1%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GCP Cloud Run scales to zero between corporate working hours; activity ingestion peaks at 8-9am and 12-1pm. Redis Memorystore M2 handles 100 concurrent active challenges with sorted set operations. MongoDB Atlas M30 auto-scales storage. Estimated cost: $400/month for 500 employees; enterprise clients with >10K employees upgrade to M50 Atlas cluster. Challenge leaderboard Redis ZADD is O(log N) — scales linearly with participant count.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (complete): manual activity logging, basic step challenge, Firebase Auth, MongoDB Atlas, GCP Pub/Sub, CircleCI CI/CD. Phase 2 (complete): wearable sync (Apple Health, Google Fit), reward points catalog, FCM push notifications, HR admin program configuration. Phase 3 (this round): resolve ISS-001 with full GDPR erasure saga and 30-day SLA monitor; resolve ISS-002 with server-side minimum group size enforcement; add offline sync queue with drift SQLite; complete DPIA documentation for legal review; production launch to pilot corporate clients.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode across all services. ESLint with import/no-cycle to prevent circular service dependencies. Jest 90% coverage gate in CircleCI. Each Pub/Sub subscriber must be idempotent — use MongoDB upsert with event_id as idempotency key. Offline sync queue must handle deduplication: POST /activities/batch checks (employee_id, logged_at) uniqueness before insert. HR admin API responses must pass through a stripIndividualFields middleware that removes employee_id from aggregate response bodies.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Pub/Sub message ordering not guaranteed — challenge-service must handle out-of-order ActivityLogged events by storing current_progress as cumulative total, not delta. Risk: GDPR erasure saga partial failure — erasure-dlq and compliance alerts mitigate but a compensating transaction framework is not implemented (acceptable for current scale). Tradeoff: MongoDB document model over relational — flexible schema for variable activity types but $group aggregations for HR analytics are less performant than SQL GROUP BY at very high employee counts. Tradeoff: drift SQLite offline queue — adds mobile app complexity but required for gym environments with poor connectivity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: GDPR erasure saga choreography fully designed with failure handling — resolves ISS-001. Server-side minimum group size of 5 enforced in challenge-service and HR admin analytics — resolves ISS-002. Offline queue conflict resolution: last-write-wins on (employee_id, logged_at) deduplication key. Consent model confirmed as opt-in at onboarding with employer unable to mandate participation.

metadata.notes: Round 3 closing GDPR erasure saga design and HR admin data boundary enforcement gaps.

project_goal.value: Build and operate a scalable ML training pipeline for a sports analytics company using Kubernetes job scheduling, GPU node pools, versioned model artifacts in S3, and automated experiment tracking to support weekly model retraining for in-game win probability models
project_goal.rationale: Core ML infrastructure need for operationalizing win probability model retraining

target_users.value: ML engineers and data scientists building and deploying win probability models
target_users.rationale: Internal technical team operating the pipeline

project_class.rationale: Platform infrastructure for ML training — no application UI

capabilities.rationale: Kubernetes GPU job scheduling, Python training scripts, feature dataset versioning, batch retraining

complexity_level.rationale: GPU job scheduling, model registry integration, A/B shadow evaluation, DVC feature versioning

risk_level.rationale: Internal tooling; model quality risk managed via accuracy gating and shadow evaluation

data_sensitivity.rationale: Game outcome and feature data is proprietary but not personally identifiable or financial

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline accessed only by internal ML engineers via GCP IAM

access_model.value: GCP IAM with Workload Identity for Kubernetes service accounts; ML engineers access MLflow and Kubeflow UI via IAP-protected endpoints
access_model.rationale: Kubernetes workload identity eliminates static service account keys

feature_scope.value: Weekly automated retraining pipeline, GPU training job scheduling on GKE, model accuracy gating, MLflow experiment tracking, DVC feature dataset versioning, S3 model artifact registry, 48-hour A/B shadow evaluation before production promotion, automated rollback on error rate spike
feature_scope.rationale: Full MLOps pipeline capability

mvp_scope.value: Weekly retraining Kubeflow pipeline, GPU training job, MLflow model registry, accuracy gating; manual promotion to production
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP proves retraining automation before adding shadow evaluation

security_baseline.value: Workload Identity for all Kubernetes service accounts; no static service account keys; GCS bucket access via IAM conditions; MLflow and Kubeflow UIs protected by IAP
security_baseline.rationale: Zero static credentials in containerized training jobs

frontend_stack.value: N/A — infrastructure project with no custom frontend; Kubeflow Pipelines UI and MLflow UI serve as operator interfaces
frontend_stack.rationale: No custom frontend development required

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Kubeflow Pipelines SDK; training scripts use PyTorch 2.2
backend_stack.rationale: Kubeflow SDK for pipeline DAG authoring; PyTorch for model training

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for pipeline metadata and feature catalog; GCS for feature datasets and model artifacts; DVC for dataset version control
data_platform.rationale: Structured metadata in PostgreSQL; DVC tracks dataset lineage against GCS objects

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot with GPU node pool (NVIDIA T4) in GCP us-central1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Kubernetes with on-demand GPU nodes; Autopilot manages node provisioning

privacy_retention_policy.value: Model artifacts retained indefinitely in GCS (versioned); feature datasets retained 3 years; MLflow experiment metadata retained indefinitely
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Long retention required for model reproducibility audits

future_scope.value: Multi-sport model support, online feature store integration, champion-challenger automated promotion without shadow period, hyperparameter optimization via Katib
future_scope.rationale: MLOps maturity roadmap

constraints.value: GPU nodes must auto-provision and de-provision to avoid idle GPU cost; training job must complete within 6 hours; model accuracy must exceed incumbent by > 0.5% AUC before promotion
constraints.rationale: Cost efficiency and quality gate requirements

observability_baseline.value: MLflow for experiment tracking; Grafana + Prometheus for pipeline infrastructure metrics; training job GPU utilization monitored via Prometheus DCGM exporter
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard MLOps observability stack

execution_preference.value: GitLab CI for pipeline code deployment; Kubeflow Pipeline runs triggered by EventBridge weekly schedule and on-demand by ML engineers
execution_preference.rationale: Consistent with company GitLab adoption

project.goal: Weekly ML retraining pipeline for win probability models with GPU scheduling and model registry

backend.framework: Kubeflow Pipelines SDK for DAG authoring; PyTorch 2.2 training scripts

security.auth: GCP Workload Identity for Kubernetes service accounts

data.storage: GCS for feature datasets and model artifacts

devops.hosting: GKE Autopilot with GPU node pool (NVIDIA T4)

constraints.quality_gate: AUC improvement > 0.5% over incumbent before promotion

reasoner_reviews.product: The 48-hour shadow evaluation window before production promotion is appropriately conservative for a win probability model. The A/B shadow evaluation must define what 'error rate spike' means concretely — presumably win probability prediction divergence > N% vs. the incumbent on live game data. This threshold must be parameterized in the pipeline config.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Kubeflow Pipelines on GKE Autopilot is the right choice — Autopilot handles GPU node provisioning automatically without manual node pool management. The critical gap from round 1: the shadow evaluation step was described but the mechanism for routing live game data to both incumbent and challenger models simultaneously was not specified. The shadow evaluator needs access to the production inference service's prediction logs, not just offline test data.
reasoner_reviews.security: Workload Identity annotation on the training-job service account must specify the GCS bucket IAM condition by prefix — training jobs should only write to models/{model_version}/ prefix, not overwrite artifacts from other versions. MLflow tracking server must be configured with PostgreSQL backend (not SQLite) to support concurrent ML engineer access.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GPU auto-provisioning on GKE Autopilot is triggered by pending pods — training jobs must specify nvidia.com/gpu resource requests in the job spec for Autopilot to provision a T4 node. Document that Autopilot T4 node cold start takes 3-5 minutes and must be accounted for in the 6-hour training budget.

backend.service_design: Kubeflow Pipeline DAG with components: fetch_feature_dataset (DVC pull from GCS), validate_features (schema and freshness checks), train_model (PyTorch training on GPU pod), evaluate_model (AUC computation on holdout), register_model (MLflow registration if AUC gate passed), shadow_evaluate (48h A/B shadow job), promote_model (update production model pointer in GCS/MLflow)
backend.background_jobs: Weekly pipeline triggered by Cloud Scheduler; on-demand trigger via GitLab CI pipeline_run job. Shadow evaluation runs as a long-running Kubernetes Job (not GPU-required) consuming live game event stream from Pub/Sub for 48 hours. Nightly data freshness check validates feature_datasets are updated within last 24h.
backend.failure_handling: Training job failure: Kubeflow retries up to 2 times; on final failure sends Slack alert to #ml-ops channel with training logs URL. Accuracy gate failure: pipeline marks run as failed, publishes MetricsBelowThreshold event, no model registered. Shadow evaluation spike: rollback to previous production model version by updating GCS pointer.

security.auth_design: training-job-sa Kubernetes service account annotated with Workload Identity binding to GCP service account ml-training@project.iam; IAM condition restricts GCS write to gs://models/model_name/{run_id}/ prefix only
security.secrets_management: No static credentials; PostgreSQL password for MLflow backend stored in GCP Secret Manager, injected via Kubernetes Secret mounted as env var in MLflow deployment

data.entities: training_jobs (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, pipeline_run_id TEXT, model_name TEXT, feature_dataset_version TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP, status TEXT, training_duration_s INT, gpu_node_type TEXT}). model_versions (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, model_name TEXT, version TEXT, auc_score FLOAT, incumbent_auc FLOAT, promoted_at TIMESTAMP, shadow_eval_start TIMESTAMP, shadow_eval_end TIMESTAMP, production_pointer_updated_at TIMESTAMP}). feature_datasets (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, name TEXT, version TEXT, dvc_commit_hash TEXT, gcs_path TEXT, row_count INT, feature_schema_version INT, created_at TIMESTAMP}). evaluation_metrics (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, model_version_id UUID FK, split TEXT CHECK split IN ('holdout','shadow'), metric_name TEXT, value FLOAT, evaluated_at TIMESTAMP}). deployment_targets (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, model_name TEXT, current_production_version TEXT, last_updated_at TIMESTAMP}).
data.storage_design: GCS bucket gs://sports-analytics-models: model artifacts versioned under models/{model_name}/{run_id}/model.pt; feature datasets as Parquet under features/{dataset_name}/{dvc_hash}/. DVC remote pointing to gs://sports-analytics-features. MLflow tracking server backed by PostgreSQL 16 (not SQLite).
data.data_risks: DVC lock file conflicts when two ML engineers run on-demand pipeline simultaneously — enforce single concurrent run per model_name via Kubeflow Pipeline concurrency policy.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster with spot GPU node pool (NVIDIA T4, 16GB VRAM); training pods request 1 T4 GPU and 32GB RAM. Kubeflow Pipelines running on dedicated ml-pipelines namespace. MLflow server deployed as GKE Deployment in mlflow namespace backed by CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16. GCS buckets for model artifacts and feature datasets. Cloud Scheduler for weekly trigger. Pub/Sub topic live-game-events consumed by shadow evaluator.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: lint (ruff), type check (mypy), pytest for pipeline component unit tests, Docker build of training image (pushed to GCP Artifact Registry), Kubeflow Pipeline YAML compilation and upload to pipeline registry. Manual approval gate before triggering on-demand production run.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus with DCGM exporter for GPU utilization on training nodes (target > 80%). Grafana dashboards: training job duration, GPU utilization, AUC trend over pipeline runs, shadow evaluation divergence metric. MLflow UI for experiment comparison. Alerts: training job > 5h (P2 warning), GPU utilization < 50% for > 30min (P3 cost alert).

previous_plan.title: Sports Analytics ML Training Pipeline v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Weekly Kubeflow Pipeline DAG on GKE Autopilot GPU node pool retraining win probability models with DVC feature versioning, MLflow registry, and accuracy gating.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Kubeflow Pipelines on GKE Autopilot; GPU training pods (NVIDIA T4); DVC pulling feature datasets from GCS; MLflow for experiment tracking and model registry; 48-hour shadow evaluation before production promotion.
previous_plan.workflows: Weekly Cloud Scheduler triggers Kubeflow pipeline: fetch features, validate, train, evaluate AUC, register if gate passed, shadow evaluate 48h, promote if no error spike.
previous_plan.data_model: training_jobs, model_versions, feature_datasets, evaluation_metrics in PostgreSQL; model artifacts in GCS.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve the shadow evaluation routing gap by designing the shadow-evaluator Kubernetes Job that subscribes to the live-game-events Pub/Sub topic, calls both the incumbent production inference service and the challenger model endpoint simultaneously, stores divergence metrics, and triggers rollback if win probability divergence exceeds 5 percentage points on more than 10% of predictions over a 4-hour window. Also must specify the MLflow PostgreSQL backend explicitly in the deployment design.

fix_report.action_taken: Designed shadow-evaluator as a long-running Kubernetes Job (no GPU required) that subscribes to live-game-events Pub/Sub topic during the 48-hour evaluation window: for each game event, calls incumbent inference endpoint (GET /v1/predict?model=production) and challenger endpoint (GET /v1/predict?model=challenger) with identical game state payload; computes |incumbent_prob - challenger_prob| divergence; writes to evaluation_metrics table with split='shadow'; if divergence > 0.05 on > 10% of predictions in any 4-hour sliding window, publishes ShadowEvaluationFailed event triggering rollback. On 48h completion with no failure, publishes ShadowEvaluationPassed triggering promote_model pipeline step.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Live game data drives shadow evaluation with quantified divergence threshold; rollback triggered automatically on significant prediction drift

fix_report.action_taken: Specified MLflow tracking server with MLFLOW_BACKEND_STORE_URI=postgresql+psycopg2://mlflow:secret@cloudsql-proxy:5432/mlflow_db in GKE Deployment environment variables; CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16 instance (db-g1-small) provisioned for MLflow metadata only; CloudSQL Auth Proxy sidecar injected into MLflow pod for secure connection without static credentials.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Multiple ML engineers can write experiment runs concurrently to MLflow without SQLite file lock errors

target_output.title: Sports Analytics ML Training Pipeline — Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: A weekly automated ML training pipeline on GKE Autopilot with on-demand NVIDIA T4 GPU node provisioning, using Kubeflow Pipelines to orchestrate feature dataset fetching via DVC, PyTorch model training, AUC accuracy gating, MLflow experiment tracking (PostgreSQL-backed), and a 48-hour shadow evaluation phase where a shadow-evaluator Kubernetes Job routes live game events from Pub/Sub to both incumbent and challenger inference endpoints, triggering automated rollback if win probability divergence exceeds configurable thresholds.
target_output.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1 with two namespaces: ml-pipelines (Kubeflow Pipelines, training jobs) and mlflow (MLflow tracking server backed by CloudSQL PostgreSQL via Auth Proxy). Training pipeline DAG: Cloud Scheduler weekly trigger -> Kubeflow Pipeline run -> five components: fetch_feature_dataset (DVC pull), validate_features, train_model (GPU pod, PyTorch), evaluate_model (AUC on holdout), register_model (MLflow if AUC gate passed), shadow_evaluate (48h Kubernetes Job consuming Pub/Sub live-game-events), promote_model (update deployment_targets in PostgreSQL and GCS production pointer). Model artifacts stored in versioned GCS paths. DVC tracks feature dataset lineage.
target_output.technology_stack: Pipeline orchestration: Kubeflow Pipelines 2.1 on GKE Autopilot. Training framework: PyTorch 2.2 with CUDA 12.1 on NVIDIA T4 GPU nodes. Feature versioning: DVC 3.x with GCS remote. Experiment tracking: MLflow 2.11 with PostgreSQL 16 backend (CloudSQL). Infrastructure as code: Terraform 1.8. CI/CD: GitLab CI. Monitoring: Prometheus + Grafana + DCGM GPU exporter. Event streaming: GCP Pub/Sub (live-game-events topic). Python 3.12 for all pipeline components.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Weekly Retraining: Cloud Scheduler triggers Kubeflow pipeline run every Monday 3am; pipeline pulls latest feature dataset version via DVC, validates schema and row count freshness (< 7 days), launches GPU training pod with PyTorch training script, computes holdout AUC, registers model in MLflow if AUC > incumbent + 0.005. Shadow Evaluation: 48-hour Kubernetes Job consumes live-game-events Pub/Sub, calls incumbent and challenger endpoints in parallel, computes divergence, evaluates rollback threshold. Model Promotion: on shadow evaluation pass, promote_model component updates deployment_targets.current_production_version and writes model.pt to gs://sports-analytics-models/production/{model_name}/latest/. Experiment Comparison: MLflow UI shows all runs with AUC, training duration, feature dataset version for ML engineer review.
target_output.system_components: Kubeflow Pipelines server (ml-pipelines namespace). Training pod (GPU pod, nvidia.com/gpu: 1, 32GB RAM, NVIDIA T4 provisioned by Autopilot). shadow-evaluator Kubernetes Job (no GPU, subscribes live-game-events Pub/Sub, calls inference endpoints, writes evaluation_metrics). MLflow tracking server (mlflow namespace, GKE Deployment, CloudSQL Auth Proxy sidecar, MLFLOW_BACKEND_STORE_URI pointing to CloudSQL PostgreSQL). CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16 db-g1-small for MLflow metadata + pipeline metadata + deployment_targets. GCS buckets: gs://sports-analytics-models, gs://sports-analytics-features. DVC remote at gs://sports-analytics-features. Cloud Scheduler (weekly trigger). GCP Pub/Sub topic live-game-events.
target_output.workflows: Weekly Retraining Pipeline: Cloud Scheduler fires Monday 3am; Kubeflow pipeline starts; fetch_feature_dataset component pulls DVC-versioned Parquet files from GCS; validate_features checks row count > 50K and feature schema version matches expected; train_model pod requests T4 GPU (Autopilot provisions node, 3-5 min cold start); PyTorch training runs up to 6 hours; evaluate_model computes AUC on 20% holdout; if AUC <= incumbent + 0.005 pipeline marks failure and sends Slack alert; if gate passed, register_model logs run to MLflow and writes model.pt to GCS run path. Shadow Evaluation: shadow-evaluator Job starts on model registration; subscribes to live-game-events Pub/Sub; for each game state event calls incumbent (GET /v1/predict?model=production) and challenger (GET /v1/predict?model=challenger) with identical game state JSON; stores both predictions and divergence in evaluation_metrics with split='shadow'; evaluates 4-hour sliding window — if |incumbent - challenger| > 0.05 on > 10% of predictions, publishes ShadowEvaluationFailed; Kubeflow pipeline consume and updates deployment_targets to previous version. On 48h with no failure, promote_model runs.
target_output.data_model: training_jobs (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, pipeline_run_id TEXT, model_name TEXT, feature_dataset_version TEXT, dvc_commit_hash TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP, status TEXT, training_duration_s INT, gpu_node_type TEXT, auc_score FLOAT}). model_versions (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, model_name TEXT, mlflow_run_id TEXT, version_tag TEXT, auc_score FLOAT, incumbent_auc_at_registration FLOAT, promoted_at TIMESTAMP, shadow_eval_start TIMESTAMP, shadow_eval_end TIMESTAMP, production_pointer_updated_at TIMESTAMP}). feature_datasets (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, name TEXT, version TEXT, dvc_commit_hash TEXT, gcs_path TEXT, row_count INT, feature_schema_version INT, created_at TIMESTAMP}). evaluation_metrics (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, model_version_id UUID FK, split TEXT CHECK split IN ('holdout','shadow'), metric_name TEXT, value FLOAT, evaluated_at TIMESTAMP, game_event_id TEXT}). deployment_targets (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, model_name TEXT, current_production_version TEXT, rollback_version TEXT, last_updated_at TIMESTAMP}). gpu_node_pools (PostgreSQL: {id UUID, node_type TEXT, provisioned_at TIMESTAMP, deprovisioned_at TIMESTAMP, training_job_id UUID FK}).
target_output.api_design: No public API — internal infrastructure. Operator interfaces: Kubeflow Pipelines UI (IAP-protected) for pipeline run history and DAG visualization. MLflow UI (IAP-protected) for experiment comparison and model registry. GitLab CI job for on-demand pipeline trigger. Internal inference endpoints consumed by shadow-evaluator: GET /v1/predict?model=production&game_id={id} and GET /v1/predict?model=challenger&game_id={id}. Shadow-evaluator calls both endpoints within 500ms; if either times out, the prediction pair is discarded (not counted toward divergence threshold).
target_output.security_and_compliance: GCP Workload Identity: training-job-sa Kubernetes service account bound to ml-training@project.iam GCP service account; IAM condition restricts GCS write to gs://sports-analytics-models/{model_name}/{pipeline_run_id}/ prefix. shadow-evaluator-sa bound to shadow-eval@project.iam with read access to inference service and write to evaluation_metrics database. MLflow server uses CloudSQL Auth Proxy sidecar — no static DB password in environment variables (mounted from GCP Secret Manager as Kubernetes Secret). Kubeflow and MLflow UIs protected by GCP IAP — only internal ML engineers with GCP identity can access. No static service account key files in any container image or CI configuration.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform manages: GKE Autopilot cluster, GPU node pool taints/tolerations, CloudSQL PostgreSQL (MLflow + pipeline metadata), GCS buckets with versioning enabled, Pub/Sub topic and subscriptions, Cloud Scheduler job, IAP configuration for Kubeflow and MLflow UIs. GitLab CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy, pytest component unit tests, Docker build (training image with PyTorch 2.2 + CUDA 12.1 pushed to Artifact Registry), Kubeflow Pipeline YAML compiled and uploaded to KFP pipeline registry via kfp SDK. Environments: dev (shared GKE cluster, no GPU — training jobs use CPU for smoke tests), staging (GPU node pool enabled), production (GPU node pool, Cloud Scheduler weekly run).
target_output.observability: Prometheus DCGM exporter on GPU training nodes: GPU utilization (target > 80% during training), VRAM usage, GPU temperature. Grafana dashboards: weekly pipeline success/failure rate, AUC trend across runs (detect model decay), shadow evaluation divergence distribution, feature dataset freshness (hours since last update). MLflow run comparison for ML engineers: AUC, training_duration_s, feature_dataset_version, hyperparameter values. PagerDuty: training job > 5h (P2), AUC gate failure (P3 — informational), shadow evaluation failure (P2 — production impact), GPU utilization < 50% for > 1h (P3 — cost alert).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: NVIDIA T4 spot node on GKE Autopilot: ~$0.35/hr; 6-hour training job = ~$2.10 per run; 52 weeks = ~$109/year for GPU. CloudSQL db-g1-small: ~$9/month. GCS storage: model artifacts ~50MB per version, 52 versions/year = ~2.6GB, negligible cost. Shadow evaluator Job runs on standard Autopilot nodes: ~$0.05 for 48h at minimal CPU. Total infrastructure: ~$150/month. GPU nodes auto-deprovision after training job completion — zero idle GPU cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (complete): Kubeflow pipeline DAG, GPU training job with PyTorch, DVC feature dataset integration, MLflow PostgreSQL-backed tracking, AUC accuracy gate, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (this round): resolve ISS-001 with shadow-evaluator Kubernetes Job design and Pub/Sub dual-inference routing; resolve ISS-002 with CloudSQL Auth Proxy for MLflow backend; add deployment_targets table for production model pointer management; add DCGM GPU observability dashboards. Phase 3: multi-sport model support (parameterize model_name in pipeline), Katib hyperparameter optimization integration, online feature store connector for sub-hour feature freshness.
target_output.development_guidelines: Kubeflow Pipeline components are pure Python functions decorated with @component — no side effects outside defined input/output artifacts. Each component unit-tested with mock GCS and mock MLflow client in pytest. training image pinned to pytorch:2.2.0-cuda12.1-cudnn8-runtime — never use :latest tag. DVC lock file committed to Git on every feature dataset update — no manual DVC pull without committed lockfile. Shadow evaluator implements idempotent Pub/Sub message processing using game_event_id as deduplication key — Pub/Sub at-least-once delivery must not double-count divergence metrics.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GKE Autopilot T4 node cold start (3-5 min) counts against 6-hour training budget — mitigate by keeping a warm node during Monday morning window via a lightweight placeholder pod. Risk: Pub/Sub message backlog if shadow evaluator falls behind live event rate during high-frequency game windows — add subscription backlog alarm and evaluator horizontal scaling via KEDA. Tradeoff: Kubeflow Pipelines over Airflow — tighter GKE integration and native GPU job support, but steeper learning curve for data scientists unfamiliar with KFP SDK. Tradeoff: CloudSQL for MLflow over self-managed PostgreSQL in GKE — managed HA and backups at higher cost, correct for production MLOps.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Shadow evaluation dual-inference routing designed via Pub/Sub and parallel inference endpoint calls — resolves ISS-001. MLflow PostgreSQL backend specified via CloudSQL Auth Proxy — resolves ISS-002. Shadow evaluation rollback threshold confirmed: divergence > 0.05 on > 10% of predictions in any 4-hour sliding window. On-demand pipeline trigger: GitLab CI job_run CI stage calls kfp.Client().create_run_from_pipeline_func() for ad hoc ML engineer runs.

metadata.notes: Round 2 sports analytics ML pipeline; resolves shadow evaluation routing and MLflow backend gaps.

profile.domain: A Windows desktop application for professional cycling team directeurs sportifs to manage race-day tactical decisions: displaying live rider GPS positions and power output from team radios, annotating race maps with strategy notes, and sending pre-composed message templates to riders via the team communication system.

project_goal.value: Windows desktop app for cycling team directeurs sportifs displaying live GPS and power telemetry for each rider, supporting tactical map annotations, and sending pre-composed radio messages to riders via team communication gateway
project_goal.rationale: Team currently uses paper maps and verbal coordination; digital tactical dashboard reduces errors during time-critical race decisions

target_users.value: Professional cycling team directeur sportif and assistant DS managing riders during race stages
target_users.rationale: 1-3 DS staff per team car; need live situational awareness of all 9 riders

project_class.rationale: WPF Windows desktop app installed in team car; no cloud hosting

capabilities.rationale: WPF UI, embedded C# SignalR hub, SQLite data, real-time telemetry feed, serial communication gateway integration

complexity_level.rationale: Real-time telemetry rendering, SVG race profile map, serial interface integration, offline PDF export

risk_level.rationale: Tactical decisions are time-sensitive; data loss or app crash during race stage is a medium business risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Rider power and GPS data, tactical notes are confidential team strategy; no personal PII beyond rider names

external_exposure.rationale: App runs in team car on isolated laptop; telemetry received via local TCP socket from race radio unit

access_model.value: Custom JWT: DS and assistant DS login with username/password; roles: ds (full access), assistant_ds (view and annotate, no message sending), admin (user management)
access_model.rationale: Message sending to riders must be restricted to authorised DS personnel; tactical notes should be attributable

feature_scope.value: Live SVG race profile with rider GPS markers, power output display per rider, tactical map annotation (attack zone, danger segment, feed zone markers), pre-composed radio message templates with keyboard shortcut triggers, communication log, post-stage PDF race report export
feature_scope.rationale: Full tactical workflow identified in DS user research sessions

mvp_scope.value: Live GPS map with 9 rider markers, power output badges, tactical annotation markers, radio message template sending, communication log
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for first race deployment at team training camp

security_baseline.value: JWT auth, no credentials stored on disk, tactical annotations and communication logs stored locally only
security_baseline.rationale: Team strategy is confidential; laptop must require login on resume

frontend_stack.value: WPF with C# (.NET 8)
frontend_stack.rationale: WPF provides native Windows performance for SVG map rendering and real-time UI updates at 5s intervals

backend_stack.value: C# .NET 8 with SignalR (embedded)
backend_stack.rationale: SignalR hub on localhost provides real-time push from telemetry TCP listener to WPF UI via WebSocket

data_platform.value: SQLite for race stage data
data_platform.rationale: Portable SQLite file per stage; exported as part of post-stage PDF report package

hosting_target.value: Windows desktop binary in team car laptop
hosting_target.rationale: Installed on team car ruggedised laptop; no cloud hosting

privacy_retention_policy.value: Race stage data retained locally for 2 years for team performance analysis; no personal data beyond rider names
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Team performance analysis requires historical GPS and power data

future_scope.value: Heart rate monitor integration, rival team GPS overlay (from race organiser feed), predictive finishing time model
future_scope.rationale: Post-season enhancement requests from DS team

constraints.value: Must operate on Windows 11 laptop in car with 4G hotspot; no guaranteed internet; core functions offline-capable; serial port for radio gateway
constraints.rationale: Team car has intermittent 4G; telemetry comes from local radio unit on serial port not internet

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting; local stage.log with structured JSON; no telemetry on tactical data
observability_baseline.rationale: Support team needs crash visibility for race-day issues; tactical data must not leave the laptop

execution_preference.value: Pre-season deployment with DS team testing at training camp before race debut
execution_preference.rationale: Race-critical software requires dry-run validation in training environment

project.goal: Live race tactical dashboard with GPS map, power telemetry, annotations, and radio message templates

frontend.map: SVG race profile with rider marker overlay

backend.telemetry: TcpListener reading race radio unit payload

security.auth: Custom JWT (System.IdentityModel.Tokens.Jwt)

data.entities: riders, race_stages, gps_positions, power_samples, tactical_annotations, message_templates, communication_logs

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: dotnet test, dotnet publish self-contained
devops.packaging: dotnet publish self-contained Windows x64 binary

reasoner_reviews.product: The tactical annotation workflow is the highest-value feature for DS users. Markers must be placeable on the SVG race profile by clicking on a specific kilometre mark, not by typing coordinates. The keyboard shortcut radio message system must be configurable per race stage since teams compose stage-specific messages (e.g., 'Attack at km 87' is race-specific). Communication log with timestamps is essential for post-race debrief.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: SignalR hub on localhost is correct for pushing telemetry updates to the WPF MVVM viewmodels. The telemetry TCP listener must run as a BackgroundService and push to SignalR which the WPF client subscribes to via HubConnection. The SVG race profile should load a stage-specific SVG file and rider positions are overlaid as SVG circle elements calculated from GPS coordinates mapped to the race profile distance axis.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT stored in-memory only; Windows DPAPI available if credential persistence is needed in future. Serial port access for radio gateway does not require elevated privileges on Windows when user is in the dialup users group; document this setup requirement. Communication log should be append-only to prevent post-race alteration of tactical records.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: System.IO.Ports.SerialPort is available in .NET 8 on Windows. Self-contained dotnet publish creates a single-folder deployment without requiring .NET runtime on the team laptop. Sentry DSN must scrub any event containing rider power values or GPS coordinates.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan did not specify how the SVG race profile maps GPS latitude/longitude coordinates to the SVG x-axis (which represents race distance in kilometres). Without a concrete GPS-to-km projection approach the live map will not render correctly for real race course data.

backend.service_design: Embedded ASP.NET Core host in WPF app (HostBuilder in App.xaml.cs). Services: TelemetryListenerService (BackgroundService reading TCP socket from race radio unit), SignalRHub (IRaceHub interface with OnTelemetryUpdate method), RiderService (SQLite CRUD for riders and stage assignments), AnnotationService (SQLite CRUD for tactical_annotations), RadioGatewayService (SerialPort wrapper for sending message templates).
backend.api_patterns: WPF MVVM ViewModels subscribe to SignalR hub via Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Client.HubConnection. TelemetryListenerService parses binary telemetry payload (rider_id uint8, lat float32, lon float32, power_watts uint16, timestamp uint32) and calls hub.SendAsync('OnTelemetryUpdate', riderUpdate). RadioGatewayService.SendMessage(template_id) serialises pre-composed message to serial frame format.
backend.failure_handling: TelemetryListenerService reconnects TCP on 5s backoff. HubConnection.Closed event triggers automatic reconnect with exponential backoff. SerialPort IOException captured; error displayed in communication log panel as 'Radio gateway disconnected'.
backend.scaling_notes: 9 riders sending telemetry every 5s generates ~1.8 GPS positions per second; trivial for SignalR single-client hub. SQLite handles per-stage data without partitioning.

frontend.app_structure: WPF MVVM with ViewModels: MainWindowViewModel (stage selection, login), RaceMapViewModel (SVG map + rider marker binding), RiderPanelViewModel (per-rider power and GPS status), AnnotationViewModel (marker placement), RadioViewModel (message template list + shortcut mapping), CommunicationLogViewModel (append-only log).
frontend.pages_and_flows: MainWindow: side panel with 9 rider badges showing name, current power (colour-coded: green >300W, yellow 200-300W, red <200W), last GPS timestamp. SVG race profile in centre pane with rider circle markers at projected km position. Annotation toolbar with marker type selector (attack/danger/feed). RadioPanel: pre-composed message list with keyboard shortcut badges; pressing shortcut calls RadioGatewayService.SendMessage and logs to communication_logs.
frontend.state_management: MVVM with CommunityToolkit.Mvvm ObservableProperty. RiderMarkerCollection as ObservableCollection<RiderMarkerViewModel> data-bound to SVG overlay ItemsControl. SignalR OnTelemetryUpdate dispatched to Dispatcher.InvokeAsync to update ObservableProperty on UI thread.
frontend.performance_notes: SVG rider markers updated via property binding; no full canvas redraw. WPF ItemsControl with Canvas panel for marker overlay. Telemetry updates debounced at 5s presentation interval by TelemetryListenerService buffering within window.

security.auth_design: ASP.NET Core Identity-lite: users table with bcrypt hashed passwords (BCrypt.Net-Core). Login calls UserService.Authenticate returning System.IdentityModel.Tokens.Jwt SignedToken (HMACSHA256, 8h TTL for race stage duration). JWT stored in WPF app MemoryCache only. Laptop screen lock triggers app lock screen requiring re-authentication.
security.authorization_model: Role claims in JWT: ds (full), assistant_ds (no SendMessage), admin (user management). ViewModel command execution gated on ClaimsPrincipal.IsInRole check before RadioGatewayService.SendMessage call.
security.security_risks: Risk: tactical annotations and communication logs visible if laptop is unattended. Mitigation: auto-lock on 10-minute idle via WPF DispatcherTimer monitoring last input timestamp.

data.entities: riders (id INTEGER PK, name VARCHAR, number INT, bike_color VARCHAR, team_stage_id FK). race_stages (id INTEGER PK, race_name VARCHAR, stage_number INT, stage_date DATE, gpx_file_path VARCHAR, total_km FLOAT, created_at DATETIME). gps_positions (id INTEGER PK, rider_id FK, stage_id FK, lat FLOAT, lon FLOAT, power_watts INT, km_from_start FLOAT, recorded_at DATETIME). tactical_annotations (id INTEGER PK, stage_id FK, annotation_type ENUM attack/danger/feed/note, km_position FLOAT, label VARCHAR, created_by VARCHAR, created_at DATETIME). message_templates (id INTEGER PK, stage_id FK, shortcut_key VARCHAR, message_text VARCHAR, category ENUM attack/defend/nutrition/mechanical). communication_logs (id INTEGER PK, stage_id FK, template_id FK nullable, message_text VARCHAR, sent_by VARCHAR, sent_at DATETIME, delivery_status ENUM sent/failed).
data.storage_design: One SQLite file per race stage: stage_{race_name}_{stage_number}.db in %APPDATA%/ds-dashboard/stages/. gps_positions append-only (no UPDATE/DELETE after insert). communication_logs append-only. WAL mode for concurrent SignalR write and UI reads.
data.analytics_events: Post-stage: generate per-rider power summary (avg, max, time above 300W) from gps_positions for PDF race report. Tactical annotation timeline exported to PDF showing km positions of all markers.

devops.deployment_topology: Self-contained dotnet publish for Windows x64 single-folder deployment. Distributed as ZIP to team laptop; no installer required for internal deployment. Sentry DSN in appsettings.json with data scrubbing config.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: dotnet build, dotnet test (xUnit for services, no WPF UI tests), dotnet publish -r win-x64 --self-contained -o publish/, ZIP artifact to GitHub Releases.
devops.observability_stack: Sentry .NET SDK with BeforeSend hook stripping events where any breadcrumb value or exception frame contains 'lat', 'lon', 'power_watts', 'message_text'. Local stage.log via Serilog JSON sink.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous ZIP artifacts retained on GitHub Releases. SQLite schema migrations additive; rollback reads existing schema without issues.

ISS-001.title: GPS-to-km projection on SVG race profile not specified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not describe how GPS lat/lon coordinates from the telemetry feed are projected to a kilometre position on the SVG race profile x-axis. Without this the live map cannot render correctly for real race course data.

history.note: Identified in critic review: no GPS-to-km mapping logic described

ISS-002.title: Communication log not specified as append-only
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 allowed DELETE on communication_logs; round 2 plan marks it append-only for post-race debrief integrity

history.note: Communication log had no append-only constraint
Append-only noted in data_model

focus_issues.title: GPS-to-km projection on SVG race profile not specified
focus_issues.detail: No GPS-to-km projection algorithm described; live rider markers cannot be placed on SVG race profile without it.

latest_recommendations: Specify GPS-to-km projection: load GPX file for stage, compute cumulative distance along track, for each telemetry GPS point find nearest GPX trackpoint by haversine distance and return its cumulative km
Store km_from_start in gps_positions computed at ingest time by TelemetryListenerService
Map km_from_start to SVG x-axis coordinate as (km_from_start / stage_total_km) * svg_width

previous_audits.summary: Solid WPF architecture with SignalR for real-time updates but the GPS-to-SVG projection is a blocking gap for the core map feature. Communication log append-only constraint also missing.
strengths: SignalR hub on localhost with TelemetryListenerService BackgroundService is the correct real-time push pattern
WPF MVVM with CommunityToolkit ObservableProperty is appropriate for high-frequency telemetry binding
SQLite per-stage file is a clean portable data design
concerns: No GPS-to-km projection logic specified; telemetry provides lat/lon not km position
Communication log missing append-only constraint
blocking_issues: GPS-to-km projection must be specified to render live rider markers on SVG race profile
recommendations: Specify GPX-based km projection: load stage GPX, compute cumulative haversine distance per trackpoint, for each telemetry update find nearest trackpoint and read its cumulative km
Compute km_from_start at TelemetryListenerService ingest and store in gps_positions
Derive SVG x coordinate as (km_from_start / stage_total_km) * svg_viewport_width

issue_updates.title: GPS-to-km projection on SVG race profile not specified
issue_updates.detail: Plan provided no algorithm for translating GPS coordinates to SVG x position on race profile

previous_plan.title: DS Race Tactical Dashboard - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: WPF C# .NET 8 desktop app with embedded SignalR hub and TelemetryListenerService for live GPS and power updates. Round 1 did not specify GPS-to-km projection.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: WPF + SignalR embedded hub on localhost. TelemetryListenerService reads TCP telemetry. SVG race profile in WPF Canvas. SQLite per-stage.
previous_plan.data_model: riders, race_stages, gps_positions (no km_from_start), tactical_annotations, message_templates, communication_logs (no append-only constraint).
previous_plan.workflows: Telemetry received from TCP, pushed via SignalR to WPF MVVM. GPS lat/lon stored but not projected to SVG map correctly.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by specifying the GPX-based GPS-to-km projection algorithm: TelemetryListenerService loads the stage GPX file on stage start, builds a list of (cumulative_km, lat, lon) trackpoints from haversine distance accumulation, and for each incoming GPS telemetry point uses a nearest-trackpoint lookup (binary search on cumulative_km after projecting to lat/lon proximity) to compute km_from_start. The km_from_start value is stored in gps_positions and used by RaceMapViewModel to compute SVG x position as (km_from_start / stage_total_km) * svg_viewport_width. Plan quality is moderate per manifest.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified GPX-based km projection in TelemetryListenerService: on stage start loads stage GPX file from race_stages.gpx_file_path, parses trackpoints into List<(double cum_km, double lat, double lon)> by accumulating haversine distances between consecutive trackpoints. For each incoming telemetry GPS update, finds nearest trackpoint by minimising haversine(telemetry_lat, telemetry_lon, tp.lat, tp.lon) and reads tp.cum_km as km_from_start. Stores km_from_start in gps_positions row at ingest time. RaceMapViewModel computes SVG x position as (km_from_start / stage.total_km) * SvgViewportWidth and updates rider marker Canvas.LeftProperty.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Live rider markers on the SVG race profile correctly track each rider's position along the race course using the pre-loaded GPX trackpoint projection, even when GPS signals have small deviations from the ideal route.

target_output.title: DS Race Tactical Dashboard
target_output.executive_summary: A WPF C# .NET 8 desktop application for professional cycling team directeurs sportifs providing a live race tactical dashboard. Rider GPS positions and power output are received via TCP socket from the team radio unit, projected to kilometre position using GPX trackpoint distance accumulation, and rendered as SVG race profile overlays updating every 5 seconds. Tactical annotations are placed on the race profile map, and pre-composed radio message templates are sent to riders via serial port gateway. Race stage data is stored in per-stage SQLite files for post-race analysis and PDF report export.
target_output.architecture_overview: Self-contained WPF .NET 8 application hosting an embedded ASP.NET Core SignalR hub on localhost:5050. TelemetryListenerService (BackgroundService) listens on TCP for the team radio unit telemetry feed, parses binary rider updates, computes km_from_start via GPX trackpoint projection, and pushes to the SignalR hub. WPF MVVM ViewModels subscribe to the hub via HubConnection and update ObservableProperty bindings for real-time rider marker positions and power badges. RadioGatewayService wraps System.IO.Ports.SerialPort for sending message templates to the team communication system. SQLite stores gps_positions, tactical_annotations, communication_logs, and message_templates for the current race stage.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: C# with .NET 8. UI: WPF with CommunityToolkit.Mvvm (ObservableProperty, RelayCommand). Real-time: Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR with HubConnection client. Database: Microsoft.Data.Sqlite with WAL mode. PDF export: QuestPDF for post-stage race report. GPS math: custom haversine implementation (no third-party GPS library). Serial: System.IO.Ports.SerialPort for radio gateway. Auth: System.IdentityModel.Tokens.Jwt with BCrypt.Net-Core. Crash reporting: Sentry .NET SDK with BeforeSend scrubbing. Logging: Serilog JSON sink to stage.log.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Live Race Map: SVG race profile loaded from stage GPX; 9 rider circle markers positioned at km_from_start projected x coordinate; updated every 5s via SignalR push; colour-coded by power zone (green >300W, yellow 200-300W, red <200W). Rider Panel: per-rider badge showing name, jersey number, last power reading, and last GPS timestamp. Tactical Annotations: click on SVG km position to place attack/danger/feed/note marker with label; stored in tactical_annotations. Radio Messages: pre-composed templates per stage with configurable keyboard shortcuts; pressing shortcut calls RadioGatewayService.SendMessage and appends to communication_logs. Communication Log: append-only timestamped list of sent messages. Post-Stage PDF: QuestPDF report with power summary per rider, tactical annotation timeline, and full communication log.
target_output.system_components: TelemetryListenerService: BackgroundService with TcpListener on configurable port; parses binary frame (1 byte rider_id, 4 bytes lat float32, 4 bytes lon float32, 2 bytes power_watts uint16, 4 bytes unix_timestamp); calls GpxProjector.ProjectToKm(lat, lon) for km_from_start; calls SignalR hub; writes gps_positions row. GpxProjector: loads GPX on stage start; builds List of (cum_km, lat, lon) trackpoints; nearest-trackpoint lookup via linear scan (250 trackpoints typical for 200km stage). RaceMapViewModel: HubConnection subscriber; updates RiderMarkerCollection ObservableCollection; computes Canvas.Left = (km / total_km) * ViewportWidth. RadioGatewayService: SerialPort 9600 baud; serialises MessageFrame; handles IOException with reconnect. AnnotationService: SQLite CRUD for tactical_annotations. PdfReportService: QuestPDF document with rider power table and annotation timeline.
target_output.workflows: Live Telemetry Rendering: TelemetryListenerService receives binary telemetry frame from TCP socket every 5s per rider; GpxProjector.ProjectToKm computes nearest GPX trackpoint cumulative km; SignalR hub broadcasts RiderTelemetryUpdate(rider_id, km_from_start, power_watts, timestamp) to all connected HubConnections; RaceMapViewModel receives update on SignalR callback thread, dispatches to Dispatcher.InvokeAsync to update RiderMarkerViewModel.KmPosition; WPF binding updates Canvas.Left to (km_from_start / total_km) * SvgViewportWidth; power_watts updates badge colour. Tactical Annotation Placement: DS clicks on SVG canvas at km position; WPF MouseLeftButtonDown handler converts canvas click X coordinate to km = (clickX / SvgViewportWidth) * total_km; AnnotationDialog opens for type and label selection; AnnotationService.CreateAnnotation writes to tactical_annotations; SVG annotation marker rendered at km position. Radio Message Send: DS presses keyboard shortcut (e.g. F3 for 'Attack now, full gas'); KeyDown handler calls RadioGatewayService.SendMessage(template_id); serial frame transmitted; communication_logs row appended with sent_at and sent_by.
target_output.data_model: riders (id INTEGER PK, name VARCHAR, jersey_number INT, bike_color VARCHAR). race_stages (id INTEGER PK, race_name VARCHAR, stage_number INT, stage_date DATE, gpx_file_path VARCHAR, total_km FLOAT, start_town VARCHAR, finish_town VARCHAR). gps_positions (id INTEGER PK, rider_id FK, stage_id FK, lat FLOAT, lon FLOAT, power_watts INT, km_from_start FLOAT, recorded_at DATETIME) - append-only. tactical_annotations (id INTEGER PK, stage_id FK, annotation_type ENUM attack/danger/feed/note, km_position FLOAT, label VARCHAR, created_by VARCHAR, created_at DATETIME). message_templates (id INTEGER PK, stage_id FK, shortcut_key VARCHAR UNIQUE, message_text VARCHAR, category ENUM attack/defend/nutrition/mechanical). communication_logs (id INTEGER PK, stage_id FK, template_id FK nullable, message_text VARCHAR, sent_by VARCHAR, sent_at DATETIME, delivery_status ENUM sent/failed) - append-only. users (id INTEGER PK, username VARCHAR UNIQUE, password_hash VARCHAR, full_name VARCHAR, role ENUM ds/assistant_ds/admin).
target_output.api_design: SignalR hub IRaceHub: OnTelemetryUpdate(RiderTelemetryUpdate) - pushed by TelemetryListenerService to subscribed WPF client. OnAnnotationCreated(AnnotationDto) - broadcast when annotation placed. Internal service APIs: GpxProjector.ProjectToKm(lat, lon) -> double (returns cumulative km from stage start). AnnotationService.CreateAnnotation(stage_id, type, km, label, user) -> Annotation. RadioGatewayService.SendMessage(template_id) -> bool (sent/failed). PdfReportService.GenerateStageReport(stage_id) -> byte[]. UserService.Authenticate(username, password) -> JwtToken. No HTTP REST endpoints; all communication via SignalR or direct service calls within host process.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT auth: BCrypt.Net-Core password hashing (work factor 12), System.IdentityModel.Tokens.Jwt HMAC-SHA256 signed token, 8h TTL matching race stage duration. JWT in-memory only; no disk persistence. Role RBAC: ds and admin roles required for RadioGatewayService.SendMessage (enforced by [Authorize(Roles='ds,admin')] on SignalR hub method). Auto-lock on 10-minute idle via WPF DispatcherTimer monitoring last user input. Sentry BeforeSend hook: drops events where any breadcrumb Data value key contains 'lat', 'lon', 'power_watts', 'message_text', 'km_from_start'. communication_logs and gps_positions are append-only by design; no DELETE SQL in any repository method.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: dotnet publish -r win-x64 --self-contained -c Release produces self-contained single-folder deployment (~120MB). Distributed as ZIP to team laptop via USB. No installer required. appsettings.json configures telemetry TCP port, serial port name, SQLite base path, Sentry DSN. GitHub Actions CI: dotnet build, dotnet test (xUnit for TelemetryListenerService, GpxProjector, AnnotationService unit tests), dotnet publish win-x64 self-contained, ZIP artifact to GitHub Releases. GPX test fixtures in test project for GpxProjector projection accuracy tests.
target_output.observability: Serilog structured JSON logger to %APPDATA%/ds-dashboard/stage.log. Log events: telemetry_received (rider_id, km_from_start, power_watts), annotation_created (type, km), message_sent (template_id, status), radio_reconnect (attempt). Sentry for unhandled exceptions with BeforeSend tactical data scrubbing. No cloud telemetry; log file reviewed by support team after race day.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost; local desktop. SQLite per-stage file grows at ~1KB per rider per minute; 250KB for a 5-hour stage with 9 riders. Post-stage PDF generated in under 5 seconds by QuestPDF for typical 200-annotation, 9-rider report.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 pre-season (8 weeks): TelemetryListenerService with GPX projection, SignalR hub, WPF race map with rider markers, power badges, JWT auth. Phase 2 (4 weeks): tactical annotation placement, radio message templates with keyboard shortcuts, communication log. Phase 3 (3 weeks): post-stage PDF report with QuestPDF, stage history browser, admin user management.
target_output.development_guidelines: C# nullable reference types enabled. CommunityToolkit.Mvvm for all ViewModels; no code-behind logic. xUnit tests for all service classes with SQLite in-memory database fixture. GpxProjector tested with real stage GPX files for projection accuracy within 500m. No WPF UI tests; manual DS validation at training camp. Sentry DSN set via environment variable in release builds; never hardcoded.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TCP telemetry socket disrupted when team car overtakes a feeding zone; TelemetryListenerService reconnects on 5s backoff; last known rider positions remain on map until next update. Risk: GPX file not loaded before stage start; TelemetryListenerService logs error and disables km projection until GPX is configured in Settings. Tradeoff: QuestPDF over RDLC reports; QuestPDF is code-first and avoids RDLC designer tooling dependency. Tradeoff: haversine nearest-trackpoint linear scan over spatial index; 250 trackpoints scanned in <1ms; spatial index adds complexity without benefit at this scale.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: GPX-based haversine projection chosen over direct lat/lon-to-SVG transform because race profiles are not linear on a map projection; GPX cumulative distance provides accurate race-course km regardless of stage geography. QuestPDF chosen over RDLC for post-stage PDF as it requires no Visual Studio report designer and produces portable PDF output. Serial port baud rate set to 9600 matching team radio gateway hardware specification.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision resolving GPS-to-km SVG projection ISS-001 for cycling team directeur sportif tactical dashboard.
